uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

enabled.description: If `true`, the account setup is customized.

accountSetupType.description: The AccountSetupType specifies which accounts will be setup during account creation with Setup Assistant.

primaryAccountManaged.description:
If `true` the primary account is used as the MDM managed account.
If `false` the local admin account is used as the MDM managed account.

primaryAccountFullName.description: The full name for the primary account to create with Setup Assistant.

primaryAccountShortName.description: The account name for the primary account to create with Setup Assistant.

allowUserNameChange.description:
If `false`, Setup Assistant disables changing the Full Name and User Name changedProperties of the primary
account.

primaryAccountAdmin.description: If `true`, Setup Assistant creates the primary account as admin user.

adminAccountFullName.description: The full name for the administrator account to create with Setup Assistant.

adminAccountShortName.description: The short name for the administrator account to create with Setup Assistant.

adminAccountHidden.description: If the administrator account should be hidden in 'Users & Groups' in macOS system preferences.

adminAccountPassword.description: The plain text password for the administrator account.

AccountSetupConfiguration.description: Represents the account configuration to create a local administrator account for macOS 11+ devices.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

defaultProfile.description:
Returns a value indicating whether this instance is the default profile.

The default profile is automatically assigned to devices during synchronization when a device has no profile assigned. Only one profile must be marked as default.

accountOrganizationName.description: The remote organization name of the DEP-Account. Not to be confused with a Relution organization.

DepAccountSyncFailure.description: Transports information about failures during a specific DEP-Account sync.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

serialNumber.description: The serial number of the macOS device.

mdmManagementUrl.description: The custom MDM management URL used for the macOS device.

MacOSDepMdmUrl.description:
Represents a temporary override for the MDM management URL for DEP-enrolled macOS devices.

This entity maps a macOS device's serial number to a custom MDM management URL that can
temporarily override the default URL during DEP enrollment. The override is valid for 24 hours
and is automatically cleaned up by a scheduled job running several times per day.

Use case:
- Allows recovery of macOS devices using the command: `sudo profiles renew -type enrollment`
even if the MDM server URL or path has changed.
- During DEP enrollment, this mapping is checked, and if an override is found, its URL is used
instead of the default one.

Note: Access to API endpoints that modify this mapping requires the authority:
`Authority.MANAGE_MDM_URLS`.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

organizationUuid.description:
To preserve the logs, the organization_uuid will set to null if an organization is deleted. 
The organization uuid represents the tenant orga, i.e. the orga that the action on that has happened. (not the orga
that user belongs to).

translationKey.description: The translation to use to explain this audit log.

translationParameters.description: A map of parameters for use in the audit log translation.

AndroidEnterpriseDeviceDetails.description: Entity to store the Google Android Enterprise (GAE) Device details.

productVersion.description: The version of the update: e.g., V1.0.0.2

AndroidOSUpdate.description: Stores information about available Android/SmartBoard updates.

localizedDescription.description: Description of the error in the device's localized language.

usEnglishDescription.description: Description of the error in US English.

AppleMDMError.description:
Holds information about MDM errors according to the MDM Protocol Reference.

Note from the protocol reference:

The ErrorDomain and ErrorCode keys contain internal codes used by Apple that may be useful for diagnostics. Your host should not rely on these values, as they may change between software releases. However, for reference, the current codes are listed in the Error Codes section of the reference.

authenticatedRootVolumeEnabled.description: If true, the system booted using an Authenticated Root Volume. This value is available in macOS 11 and later.

autoLockTime.description:
The number of seconds before a device goes to sleep after being idle. This value is only available on Shared iPad in
iOS 17 and later.

bootstrapTokenAllowedForAuthentication.description:
This value specifies whether the Secure Enclave Processor (SEP) supports and allows secure operations to use the. 
Bootstrap Token. The value is automatically set for devices enrolled through the Device Enrollment Program (DEP). The
user can also manually set this value in the RecoveryOS.
This value is available for Apple silicon in macOS 11 and later. Not available for user enrollment.
Possible Values: allowed, disallowed, not supported.

bootstrapTokenRequiredForKernelExtension.description:
If true, the device can accept a Bootstrap Token from the MDM server instead of prompting for user authentication
prior to enabling kernel extensions. This includes enabling kexts through the
com.apple.syspolicy.kernel-extension-policy payload or triggering the RestartDevice command with RebuildKernelCache
set to true. This only applies when BootstrapTokenAllowedForAuthentication is true in the. 
SecurityInfoResponse.SecurityInfo response.
This value is available for Apple silicon in macOS 11 and later. Not available for user enrollment.

bootstrapTokenRequiredForSoftwareUpdate.description:
If true, the device can accept a Bootstrap Token from the MDM server instead of prompting for user authentication
prior to installation. This only applies when BootstrapTokenAllowedForAuthentication is true in the. 
SecurityInfoResponse.SecurityInfo response.
This value is available for Apple silicon in macOS 11 and later. Not available for user enrollment.

fdeEnabled.description:
If true, the device has enabled FileVault full disk encryption (FDE). This value is available in macOS 10.9 and
later.

fdeHasInstitutionalRecoveryKey.description: If true, FileVault FDE has an institutional recovery key. This value is available in macOS 10.9 and later.

fdeHasPersonalRecoveryKey.description: If true, FileVault FDE has a personal recovery key. This value is available in macOS 10.9 and later.

fdePersonalRecoveryKeyDeviceKey.description:
If the FileVault personal recovery key has enabled escrow with a recovery key, this value is the device serial
number. This is the value that displays to the user at the EFI login window as part of the help message if they enter
their password incorrectly three times. The server also uses this value as an index when saving the device personal
recovery key. This replaces the recordNumber that the server returned in the previous escrow mechanism. This value is
available in macOS 10.13 and later.

firewallSettings.description: A dictionary that contains the firewall settings. This value is available in macOS 10.12 and later.

firmwarePasswordStatus.description: A dictionary that contains the status of the EFI firmware password. This value is available in macOS 10.13 and later.

hardwareEncryptionCaps.description:
An integer that indicates the underlying hardware encryption capabilities of the device, which is one of the
following values:
1: Block-level encryption
2: File-level encryption
3: Both block-level and file-level encryption. 
Important
For a device to have data protection, HardwareEncryptionCaps must be 3 and PasscodePresent must true.
This value is available in iOS 4 and later, and tvOS 6 and later.

isRecoveryLockEnabled.description:
If true, a password is required to enter recovery (see SetRecoveryLockCommand). Available in macOS 11.5 and later and
only on Apple silicon devices.

managementStatus.description: A dictionary that contains the status of the device’s MDM enrollment.

passcodeCompliant.description:
If true, the user’s passcode is compliant with all requirements on the device, including Exchange and other accounts.
This value is available in iOS 4 and later, and tvOS 6 and later.

passcodeCompliantWithProfiles.description:
If true, the user’s passcode is compliant with requirements from profiles. This key doesn’t apply to User-Enrolled
devices. This value is available in iOS 4 and later, and tvOS 6 and later.

passcodeLockGracePeriod.description:
The user preference for the number of seconds before a locked screen requires the device passcode to unlock it. This
value is only available for Shared iPad.

passcodeLockGracePeriodEnforced.description:
The enforced value for the number of seconds before a locked screen requires the device passcode to unlock it. If a
device has a passcode, changing PasscodeLockGracePeriod to a larger value doesn’t take effect until the user logs out
or removes the passcode. This value is only available for Shared iPad.

passcodePresent.description: If true, the device has a passcode. This value is available in iOS 4 and later, and tvOS 6 and later.

remoteDesktopEnabled.description: If true, Remote Desktop is active on the device. This value is available in macOS 10.14.4 and later.

systemIntegrityProtectionEnabled.description: If true, System Integrity Protection (SIP) is active on the device. This value is available in macOS 10.12 and later.

secureBoot.description: A dictionary that contains the device’s Secure Boot settings. This value is available in macOS 10.15 and later.

AppleSecurityInfo.description: data exposed from SecurityInfoResponse for display purposes.

blockAllIncoming.description: If true, the firewall blocks all incoming connections.

firewallEnabled.description: If true, the firewall is on.

loggingOption.description:
The type of logging emitted by the firewall.
Possible Values: throttled, brief, detail.

stealthMode.description: If true, stealth mode is active for the firewall.

changePending.description:
If true, a firmware password change is pending. A device restart is necessary for this change to take effect. Until
then, additional attempts to change the password fail.
Note. 
If true, the other values show the current state of the device, not the state after a restart.

passwordExists.description: If true, the device has an EFI firmware password.

enrolledViaDEP.description:
If true, the device enrolled in MDM through the Device Enrollment Program (DEP). This value is available in macOS
10.13.2 and later.

isActivationLockManageable.description:
If true, the type of enrollment allows the MDM to manage Activation Lock for this device. This value is available in
macOS 10.15 and later.

isUserEnrollment.description: If true, the device is user-enrolled. This value is available in iOS 13 and later, and macOS 10.15 and later.

userApprovedEnrollment.description:
If true, the enrollment was user-approved. If false, the device may reject certain security-sensitive payloads or
commands. This value is available in macOS 10.13.2 and later.

externalBootLevel.description:
The device’s external boot level, which indicates whether it allows booting from an external device, disallows it, or
doesn’t support it.
Possible Values: allowed, disallowed, not supported.

secureBootLevel.description:
The security level for the bootable operating system versions.
Possible Values: off, medium, full, not supported.

reducedSecurity.description:
Reports which security features the user disables in recoveryOS. This property is only present for Apple silicon when. 
SecureBootLevel is medium.
Available in iOS 11 and later.

allowsAnyAppleSignedOS.description: If true, allows any signed version of trusted system software from Apple to run.

allowsMDM.description: If true, the MDM server controls kernel extensions and software updates.

allowsUserKextApproval.description: If true, the user has control over kernel extensions.

supplementalBuildVersion.description: Information which is only present if an os update was executed as Rapid Security Response (RSR) on the device. See RSR documentation for further details.n Build version of the RSR update e.g. "22A771000a".

supplementalOSVersionExtra.description: Extension of build version, represented as small letter e.g. "(a)".

softwareUpdateDeviceId.description: The key to get the device identifier that you use to look up available OS updates through https://gdmf.apple.com/v2/pmv. Available in iOS 15 and later, and macOS 12 and later.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

subjectUids.description: Prepared for using an Embeddable type if we want to store more metadata than the subject UID in the future.

commonName.description:
This field is abbreviated if the actual value is longer than 512 characters. The field should mostly be used for
presentation purpose. For actual logic, is safer to load the data from resource.

distinguishedName.description:
This field is abbreviated if the actual value is longer than 4000 characters. The field should mostly be used for
presentation purpose. For actual logic, is safer to load the data from resource.

organizationalUnit.description:
This field is abbreviated if the actual value is longer than 512 characters. The field should mostly be used for
presentation purpose. For actual logic, is safer to load the data from resource.

serialNumber.description:
This field is abbreviated if the actual value is longer than 255 characters. The field should mostly be used for
presentation purpose. For actual logic, is safer to load the data from resource. The serial number is stored in
decimal format.

revocationListUuid.description:
CRL of this certificate.

The value is set once upon creation of the certificate. It is used as part of the URL coded as CRL distribution
point. Therefore this is fixed upon certificate generation.

Notice, the uuid references the CRL controlling revocation of this certificate. It is not the CRL for issued
certificates when this certificate is used a CA.

dockMacAddress.description:
Built-in MAC address for the docking station that the device connected to.
Factory sets Media access control address (MAC address) assigned for use by a dock. Currently this is only
supported on the Dell Arcada / Sarien devices and the Dell WD19 / WD19TB Docking Station. It is reserved
specifically for MAC pass through device policy. The format is twelve (12) hexadecimal digits without any
delimiter (uppercase letters). This is only relevant for Dell devices.

orgUnitId.description:
The unique ID of the organizational unit.
orgUnitPath is the human readable version of orgUnitId. While orgUnitPath may change by renaming an
organizational unit within the path, orgUnitId is unchangeable for one organizational unit. This property can be
[updated](/admin-sdk/directory/v1/guides/manage-chrome-devices#move_chrome_devices_to_ou) using the API. For more
information about how to create an organizational structure for your device, see the [administration help
center](https://support.google.com/a/answer/182433).

orgUnitPath.description:
The full parent path with the organizational unit's name associated with the device.
Path names are case insensitive. If the parent organizational unit is the top-level organization, it is
represented as a forward slash, `/`. This property can be
[updated](/admin-sdk/directory/v1/guides/manage-chrome-devices#move_chrome_devices_to_ou) using the API. For more
information about how to create an organizational structure for your device, see the [administration help
center](https://support.google.com/a/answer/182433).

ChromeOSDeviceDetailsCpuInfoLogicalCpus.description: Status of a single logical CPU.

ChromeOSDeviceDetailsCpuInfoLogicalCpusC.description:
Status of a single C-state.
C-states are various modes the CPU can transition to in order to use more or less power.

vendorSpecific.description: Vendor-specific information such as Vendor ID.

DepDetails.description: Information specific to Apple DEP devices. Empty for non-DEP devices.

DepDetailsProfile.description: subset of DepProfile data contained in DepDetails.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

user.description:
defines authorization context used for the device.

Caution: This is planned to be changed to user UUID (instead of role) with `null` representing the device
users group.

externalId.description:
The name of an AE device in the form enterprises/{enterpriseId}/devices/{deviceId}.
The value may be `null`.

model.description:
the model of the device

Note: for Apple devices, the "ProductName" field is used instead of "Model" or "ModelName".

verifySignature.description:
Property used by Apple devices to define whether the MDM signature is provided by the device and therefore must
be verified against the request body during communication between client and server or not.

awaitingConfiguration.description:
If true, the device is awaiting a Device Configured command before proceeding through Setup Assistant.

While the device is in the await configuration state, we're able to perform some setup using MDM commands.n f.ex. we're able to apply a AccountSetupConfiguration to pre-configure macOS user accounts or install managed apps that should have their data preserved during a MDM migration.

io.relution.apple.mdm.AwaitConfigurationHandler is responsible for automatically releasing the devices from the await configuration state when ready.

Admins also have the option to manually release the device via the io.relution.action.details.ReleaseFromAwaitConfigurationAction

pushUdid.description: UDID = Unique Device ID of an Apple Device.

enrollmentIdentifier.description:
Server-managed property to help identify devices during (re-)enrollment.

This property must not be reset on device withdrawal/deletion!

If applicable, values should correspond to stable device identifiers which are present during enrollment time.

  • Apple Devices: pushUdid of the device, except for user-enrollments, for which it corresponds to a random UUID (see PARAM_APPLE_ENROLLMENT_ID).
  • Windows Devices: deviceId of the device
  • Android Enterprise Devices: externalId of the device, matching one of previousDeviceNames reported by Google.
.

accountDrivenUserEnrollment.description:
Whether the iOS device was enrolled via account-driven user enrollment.

A user-enrollment device can be enrolled via profile-based enrollment or account-driven user enrollment (iOS 15+). Depending on the concrete method of enrollment, different properties need to be set in the initial MDM-Payload.

lastConnectionDate.description: Last reported time at which the device communicated via MDM - Apple & Windows: updated after signature verification passed - Android Classic (incl. IFPs): updated when the server is contacted by the agent or companion. - Android Enterprise: basically lastStatusReport - ChromeOS: lastSync.

lastDeviceDetailsRefreshDate.description:
The last time the device details have been refreshed.
Managed by the server for all platforms except Android Classic.

lastClientAppConnectionDate.description: The last time the client (Agent) has contacted the server.

lastCompanionConnectionDate.description: The last time the companion has contacted the server.

enrollmentDate.description:
The last time the device was enrolled (in millis since epoch)

For devices that allow re-enrollment, this values is updated every time the device gets re-enrolled.

For devices that can't be re-enrolled, once set, this values remains unchanged and is usually equal to creationDate.

sharedDeviceStatus.description:
Returns the shared device status. This field is transient and will only be set if the device was retrieved via
getDevice(String) or
getDevices(GetQuery).

adminAccountShortName.description:
The short name for the local administrator account configured in the AccountSetupConfiguration of the. 
DepProfile.

ignoreIdentityCertExpiration.description:
Indicates whether MDM messages signed with an expired identity certificate should be accepted.

By default, the server rejects MDM messages signed with an invalid certificate, which includes expired certificates. When this flag is set to true, the server accepts messages signed with an expired certificate, provided the certificate was issued by the server's CA and the certificate's CN is either "null" or matches the device's UDID/Apple Enrollment ID.

roaming.description:
Whether at least one of the device's (e)SIMs reported to be in roaming. Always `false` for devices without an
(e)SIM.

securedSharedDeviceStatus.description: To ensure to stay compatible with older client versions.

policies.description:
Gets the device policies that have been assigned to the device in order of their priority at the time of
assignment.

enrolledInAndroidManagement.description: Check if the device is enrolled via Google's Android Management API.

executedPolicy.description: Gets the device policy the device has reported as applied.

executedPolicies.description: Gets the device policy the device has reported as applied in order of their priority at the time of assignment.

iotDevice.description:
Returns a value indicating whether the device is an IoT device.

This method returns `true`, if the platform of the device is an IoT platform. This is the case, when the platform's category is equal to IOT.

policy.description: Gets the device policy that has been assigned to the device.

Device.description: represents a (mobile) device known to the server usually by enrollment for management.

capabilityType.description:
whether the information relates to a HARDWARE or. 
SOFTWARE component.

manufacturer.description: name of manufacturer of some device component.

model.description: name of model of device component.

revision.description: hard- or software revision in manufacturer-specific format.

DeviceCapability.description:
defines a hard- or software component of the device in terms of manufacturer, model name and revision.

This is stored as part of `IotDeviceDetails` and allows finer classification of devices.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

lostModeStatus.description:
Indicates if the device is in lost mode (ENABLED or DISABLED)
or there is a pending action to enable (ENABLE_PENDING) or disable. 
DISABLE_PENDING lost mode.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

startDate.description: Start date of the loan period.

endDate.description: End date of the loan period.

returnDate.description:
Date when the device have been returned.
If this date is set the loan has finished.

active.description: Flag indicating this is an active lending on a device, used also for sorting.

DevicePolicy.description:
Simple POJO that encapsulates certain attributes of a policy version which is
directly link to the device and its parent policy.

isDeviceSecure.description: Whether the device is secured with PIN/password.

unknownSourcesEnabled.description: Whether installing apps from unknown sources is enabled.

developmentSettingsEnabled.description: Whether developer mode is enabled on the device.

adbEnabled.description: Whether ADB is enabled on the device.

isEncrypted.description: Whether the storage encryption is enabled.

verifyAppsEnabled.description: Whether Verify Apps (Google Play Protect) is enabled on the device.

DeviceSettings.description: Information about security related device settings on device.

name.description: Name of the display.

refreshRate.description: Refresh rate of the display in frames per second.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

readOnly.description:
a role is read-only so that it can not be edited or deleted by administrators.

System dynamic permission roles corresponding to system groups are managed by the server automatically.

exportable.description: read-only roles managed by the server software are skipped upon export.

ethernetIpAddress.description: The ip address of the ethernet connection.

ethernetMacAddress.description: The ip address of the ethernet connection.

brand.description: Brand of the device. For example, Google.

hardware.description: Name of the hardware. For example, Angler.

model.description: The model of the device. For example, Asus Nexus 7.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

shortVersion.description:
The application’s short version.
Availability: Available in iOS 5.0 and later.

isValidated.description:
If true, the app has validated as allowed to run and is able to run on the device. If an app is
enterprise-distributed and is not validated, it will not run on the device until validated.
Availability: Available in iOS 9.2 and later.

flags.description:
platform-dependent flags of the installed app.

For Android these are the flags of the ApplicationInfo as defined in the Package Manager API, see
https://developer.android.com/reference/android/content/pm/ApplicationInfo.html#flags.

bundleSize.description: The app static bundle size, in bytes.

dynamicSize.description:
The size of the app document, library, and other folders, in bytes.
Availability: Available in iOS 5.0 and later.

installing.description: If `true`, the app is being downloaded. Otherwise, it’s already installed on the device.

appStoreVendable.description: If `true`, the app came from the store and can participate in store features.

deviceBasedVpp.description: If `true`, the app is distributed to the device without requiring an Apple ID.

betaApp.description: If `true`, the app is part of the Beta program.

adHocCodeSigned.description: If `true`, the app is ad-hoc code signed.

hasUpdateAvailable.description:
If `true`, the app has an update available. This key will only be present for App Store apps. On macOS,
this key will only be present for VPP apps.

installerPackageName.description: The package name of the app that installed this app.

managedAppStatus.description: The status of the managed app (managed iOS apps only).

managedAppHasConfiguration.description: If `true`, the app has a server-provided configuration (managed iOS apps only).

installScope.description: Defines the scope (device or user) in which the app is installed.

userFacingType.description: Defines whether the AE app is user facing.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

isIdentity.description: Set to true if this is an identity certificate.

serialNumber.description:
Serial number as decimal string.

The number is saved as string as it is too long for regular number types, use BigInteger if you need to do number stuff.

Notice that the serialnumber is sometimes displayed as hexadecimal number in the settings of the device.

IotDeviceDetails.description: IoT devices support a uniform way of querying the device catalog for metadata.

JsonNode.description: can be any value, including null: string, number, boolean, array or object.

LinuxDeviceDetails.description:
Details of a {@link io.relution.common.Platform#LINUX Linux device} which are computed by data retrieved from
osquery.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

course.description:
Returns the bearing at the time of this location, in degrees, or a negative value if the bearing is unknown.

Bearing is the horizontal direction of travel of a device and is unrelated to the device orientation. The bearing is in the range [0, 360).

Location.description: Contains information about a device's approximate location at a specific point in time.

mscaEntraId.description: the mapping to which MSCA Entra ID it belongs.

user.description: the mapping to which relution user it belongs.

catalogURL.description:
CatalogURL
The URL to the software update catalog currently in use by the client.

Represents the managed app information (currently applicable only for iOS apps).n

https://developer.apple.com/documentation/devicemanagement/managedapplicationlistresponse/managedapplicationlist/app_identifier

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

ManagedMedia.description:
Contains information about managed media assigned to a device (currently only applicable for iOS).

Entries are created by the managed media service in {@link ManagedMediaService#processManagedMediaResponse(String, Map) ManagedMediaService#processManagedMediaResponse(String, Map)} processManagedMediaResponse}, i.e. after a self-hosted asset has been assigned to a device and its information has been refreshed.

Managed media available in an organization is stored as a VppAsset with the type. SELF_PROVIDED.

totalRam.description: Total RAM on device in bytes.

totalInternalStorage.description: Total internal storage on device in bytes.

MemoryInfo.description: Information about device memory and storage.

MeshNodeDeviceDetails.description:
details of a native BlueRange node.

In the BlueRange mesh network a node is addressed by its nodeId. Node firmware may be updated over the air via DFU.

imei.description: IMEI number of the GSM device. For example, A1000031212.

meid.description: MEID number of the CDMA device. For example, A00000292788E1.

wifiMacAddress.description:
Wi-Fi MAC address of the device. For example, 7c:11:11:11:11:11.

This is populated from AMAPI device data, see
getWifiMacAddress().

networkOperatorName.description: Alphabetic name of current registered operator. For example, Vodafone.

telephonyInfos.description:
Telephony information associated with a given SIM card on the device. Only supported on fully managed devices
starting from Android API level 23.

ipAddress.description:
last known IP address of the device.

This is populated from application feedback, see IP.

macAddress.description:
MAC of network adapter.

This is populated from application feedback, see MAC.

NetworkInfoDetailsNetworkInterface.description: Network information from osquery tables interface_addresses and interface_details.

settingName.description: The name of the policy setting. This is the JSON field name of a top-level Policy field.

nonComplianceReason.description: The reason the device is not in compliance with the setting.

packageName.description: The package name indicating which app is out of compliance, if applicable.

requiredApp.description: The app which is identified by the packageName.

fieldPath.description: For settings with nested fields, if a particular nested field is out of compliance, this specifies the full path to the offending field. The path is formatted in the same way the policy JSON field would be referenced in JavaScript, that is: 1) For object-typed fields, the field name is followed by a dot then by a subfield name. 2) For array-typed fields, the field name is followed by the array index enclosed in brackets. For example, to indicate a problem with the url field in the externalData field in the 3rd application, the path would be applications[2].externalData.url

installationFailureReason.description:
If packageName is set and the non-compliance reason is APP_NOT_INSTALLED or. 
APP_NOT_UPDATED, the detailed reason the app can't be installed or updated.

NonComplianceDetail.description: Provides detail about non-compliance with a policy setting.

productKey.description: The product key that represents this update.

buildVersion.description: The build number of the update.

productVersion.description: The version of the update: e.g., 9.0.

lastUpdateStatusTime.description: The last time the status of this OS update was updated in millis since epoch.

targetLocalDateTime.description: Holds the target local date time of a pending declarative update.

OSUpdate.description:
Stores information about available iOS/MacOS updates as specified in the MDM Protocol Reference.

For more information look for "AvailableOSUpdates" in the MDM Protocol Reference.

.

Since this is not the real object, this can't be used for filtering and changing the members!n For this, the member and roles attributes with the sets containing the UUIDs have to be used.

androidVersion.description: The user-visible Android version string. For example, 6.0.1.

androidDevicePolicyVersionCode.description: The Android Device Policy app version code.

Build time.
A timestamp in RFC3339 UTC "Zulu" format, accurate to nanoseconds. Example: "2014-10-02T15:01:23.045123456Z".

primaryLanguageCode.description: An IETF BCP 47 language code for the primary locale on the device.

TelephonyInfo.description: Telephony information associated with a given SIM card on the device. Only supported on fully managed devices starting from Android API level 23. For more information see Android. Management API Documentation.

dataQuota.description: (Required) If present, the user’s data quota in bytes. This isn’t present if the account doesn’t enforce a quota.

dataUsed.description: (Required) The amount of data, in bytes, that the user has used.

fullName.description: (Required) The user’s full name. This value is available in macOS 10.13 and later.

hasDataToSync.description: (Required) If true, the user has data to sync to the cloud.

Gets the quality of a product in the iTunes Store.

Possible values are:

  • STDQ = Standard quality.
  • PLUS = High quality.
.

VppAsset.description:
Represents a product acquired through Apple's Volume Purchase Program (VPP).

This is the internal entity stored in the database.

A product is uniquely identified by its iTunes Store Identifier ({@link io.relution.vpp.asset.VppAsset#getAdamId ADAM ID}). Products can be apps or media, like books or music.

Number of licenses in each known VppLicenseStatus per vpp token.

entraIdUserUuid.description:
the linked relution user currently does not have to be the same as the actually used entra user on the device
therefore during enrollment we will store the linked entra user in this field. So while the user can be changed
in Relution, the linked Entra ID User will not change, and we can still track the actual linked Entra ID user.
note: the uuid we store here is the internal relution uuid of that user, not its Entra ID uuid.
should not be set outside of enrollment workflows, see
getWindowsDeviceForEnrollment.

It contains info about if this platform is enabled/disabled (onboarded) and which user groups are linked.

externalId.description: should actually always be our multi-tenant-app id, see getMultiTenantAppClientId().

CompliancePartner.description:
This entity represents an internally usable version of ComplianceManagementPartner. It contains (partially nested) information about the conditional access set up such as:
  • which platforms a third party relution admin has enabled microsoft sided
  • which groups are linked to it
  • what's the state
  • last heartbeat
  • last disaster recovery
It also is extended by data we need, like the linked tenant etc.

A policy change history can contain multiple entries.

whitelistedApps.description: Black or whitelist according to isBlacklist value.

lockTaskAllowed.description: Whether the app is allowed to lock itself in full-screen mode.

minimumVersionCode.description:
The minimum version of the app that runs on the device. If set, the device attempts to update the app to at least
this version code. If the app is not up-to-date, the device will contain a NonComplianceDetail with
nonComplianceReason set to APP_NOT_UPDATED. The app must already be published to Google Play with a version code
greater than or equal to this value. At most 20 apps may specify a minimum version code per policy.

defaultPermissionPolicy.description:
The default policy for all permissions requested by the app. If specified, this overrides the policy-level
defaultPermissionPolicy which applies to all apps. It does not override the permissionGrants which applies to all
apps.

managedConfigurationTemplate.description:
The managed configurations template for the app, saved from the managed configurations iframe. This field is
ignored if managedConfiguration is set.

managedConfiguration.description:
Managed configuration applied to the app.

See: ApplicationPolicy#managedConfiguration

.

delegatedScopes.description: The scopes delegated to the app from Android Device Policy.

connectedWorkAndPersonalApp.description:
Controls whether the app can communicate with itself across a device’s work and personal profiles, subject to user
consent.

workProfileWidgets.description: Specifies whether the app installed in the work profile is allowed to add widgets to the home screen.

installPriority.description:
Specifies the order in which the Applications should be installed in ascending order from 1-10000. 0 means that the
app has no priority. Only applies to Applications with the installType FORCE_INSTALLED or PREINSTALLED.

userControlSettings.description:
Specifies whether user control is permitted for the app.

User control includes user actions like force-stopping and clearing app data. Supported on Android 11 and above.

roles.description:
Optional. Roles the app has.Apps having certain roles can be exempted from power and background
execution restrictions, suspension and hibernation on Android 14 and above. The user control
can also be disallowed for apps with certain roles on Android 11 and above. Refer to the
documentation of each RoleType for more details.
The value may be `null`.

signingKeyCerts.description:
Optional. Signing key certificates of the app. This field is required in the following cases:
The app has installType set to CUSTOM (i.e. a custom app). The app has roles set to a nonempty
list and the app does not exist on the Play Store. The app has extensionConfig set (i.e. an
extension app) but ExtensionConfig.signingKeyFingerprintsSha256 (deprecated) is not set and the
app does not exist on the Play Store.If this field is not set for a custom app, the policy is
rejected. If it is not set when required for a non-custom app, a NonComplianceDetail with. 
INVALID_VALUE is reported.For other cases, this field is optional and the signing key
certificates obtained from Play Store are used.
The value may be `null`.

appUuid.description: The app uuid for a native app (CUSTOM).

AndroidApplicationPolicy.description:
Specifies the policy for an Android application.

The policy defines whether an app should be installed, disabled, its minimum version, how to react to runtime permission requests, its managed configuration and other parameters.

AndroidApplicationPolicyRole.description: Role type of app application configured in AndroidApplicationPolicy.

locationMode.description: The degree of location detection enabled.

connectedWorkAndPersonalApp.description:
Controls whether a single app can communicate with itself across a device’s work and personal profiles, subject to
user consent. This setting is used as default for all apps configured in this configuration.

certificateUsage.description:
Specifies whether the configuration's certificate (template) or a user based certificate should be used.

If this value is set to USER, the certificate and/or template are ignored and the certificate of the user assigned to the device the configuration is applied on is used instead.

workProfileWidgetsDefault.description:
Specifies the default behaviour for work profile widgets. If the policy does not specify
workProfileWidgets for a
specific application, it will behave according to the value specified here.

AndroidEnterpriseCrossProfilePolicy.description: Controls the data from the work profile that can be accessed from the personal profile and vice versa. A nonComplianceDetails with MANAGEMENT_MODE is reported if the device does not have a work profile. (developers.google.com)

usbDataAccess.description: Controls what files and/or data can be transferred via USB. Supported only on company-owned devices.

configureWifi.description:
Controls Wi-Fi configuring privileges.
Based on the option set, user will have either full or limited or no control in configuring Wi-Fi networks.

wifiDirectSettings.description:
Controls configuring and using Wi-Fi direct settings.
Supported on company-owned devices running Android 13 and above.

tetheringSettings.description:
Controls tethering settings.
Based on the value set, the user is partially or fully disallowed from using different forms of tethering.

wifiSsidPolicyType.description: Configures whether the Wi-Fi ssids should be restricted.

wifiSsids.description: List of Wi-Fi ssids that should either be allowed or denied.

bluetoothSharing.description: Controls whether Bluetooth sharing is allowed.

AndroidEnterpriseDevicePasswordConfigura.description: Specifies the password requirements for a device enrolled with Android Enterprise.

AndroidEnterpriseFeedbackChannelResult.description: Used to display results of Android Enterprise feedback channel as {@link FeedbackChannelComplianceViolation compliance violation}.

allowBluetoothSharing.description:
Whether bluetooth sharing is allowed.
Using a boolean instead of PersonalUsageBluetoothSharing since only. 
BLUETOOTH_SHARING_ALLOWED and
BLUETOOTH_SHARING_DISALLOWED are valid values.

host.description: The host of the direct proxy.

port.description: The port of the direct proxy.

excludedHosts.description:
For a direct proxy, the hosts for which the proxy is bypassed. The host names may contain wildcards such as *.example.com.

Multiple hosts can be separated by a newline or comma.

pacUri.description: The URI of the PAC script used to configure the proxy.

certificateType.description:
If Type disagrees with the x509 v3 basic constraints or key usage attributes, the certificateType field should be
honored.
PKCS12 field is required if certificateType is Client, otherwise ignored.
For certificates with private keys, this is the base64 encoding of the PKCS#12 file.
X509 field is required if certificateType is Server or Authority, otherwise ignored.
For certificate without private keys, this is the X509 certificate in PEM format.

scope.description: If this is given, it specifies the scope, in which the certificate should be applied.

id.description: Gets the identifier of the Chrome extension for which the certificate should be applied.

AndroidEnterpriseRelutionCertificateScop.description: Identifies the scope in which a certificate should be applied.

subscriptionCode.description: The subscription code to access the app.

userName.description: The user name of the student / teacher.

clearCookies.description: Whether Chrome, Firefox and internet cookies will be deleted.

blockListApplyDefault.description: Whether the default blocklist with be applied - https://school.tabnova.com/block.list

blockListCustom.description: The uri of a custom blocklist.

deleteFilesAndFolder.description: List of files and folder names to delete, ex. Downloads, DCIM.

appsToClearOrUninstall.description: Apps that should be have their data cleared or get uninstalled on lesson end.

clearData.description: Whether the data of the app should be cleared on lesson end.

uninstall.description: Whether the app should be uninstalled on lesson end.

name.description: Profile

systemUpdateType.description: The type of system update to configure.

startMinutes.description:
If the type is WINDOWED, the start of the maintenance window, measured as the number of
minutes after midnight in the device's local time.
This value must be between 0 and 1439, inclusive.

endMinutes.description:
If the type is WINDOWED, the end of the maintenance window, measured as the number of
minutes after midnight in device's local time.
This value must be between 0 and 1439, inclusive.
If this value is less than startMinutes, then the maintenance
window spans midnight.
If the maintenance window specified is smaller than 30 minutes, the actual window is extended to 30 minutes
beyond the start time.

freezePeriods.description:
An annually repeating time period in which over-the-air (OTA) system updates are postponed to freeze the OS
version running on a device.
To prevent freezing the device indefinitely, each freeze period must be separated by at least 60 days.

startDate.description:
The start date (inclusive) of the freeze period.
Note: year must not be set.
For example, {"month": 1,"date": 30}.

endDate.description:
The end date (inclusive) of the freeze period. Must be no later than 90 days from the start date.
If the end date is earlier than the start date, the freeze period is considered wrapping year-end.
Note: year must not be set.
For example, {"month": 1,"date": 30}.

AndroidEnterpriseSystemUpdateConfigurati.description:
A system freeze period.
When a device’s clock is within the freeze period, all incoming system updates (including security patches) are
blocked and won’t be installed.
When a device is outside the freeze period, normal update behavior applies.
Leap years are ignored in freeze period calculations, in particular:
- If Feb. 29th is set as the start or end date of a freeze period, the freeze period will start or end on Feb.
28th instead.
- When a device’s system clock reads Feb. 29th, it’s treated as Feb. 28th.
- When calculating the number of days in a freeze period or the time between two freeze periods, Feb. 29th is
ignored and not counted as a day.

Represents a partial calendar date composed of month and day, used specifically for the freeze periods as
specified by the Android Management API.

name.description: User friendly name of the wifi configuration.

autoConnect.description: Indicating that the network should be connected to automatically when in range.

hexSsid.description: Hex representation of the network's SSID.

hiddenSsid.description: Indicating if the SSID will be broadcast.

passphrase.description:
Describes the passphrase for WEP/WPA/WPA2 connections.
If WEP-PSK is used, the passphrase must be of the format 0x<hex-number>, where <hex-number> is 40, 104, 128, or
232 bits.
The Android Management API only supports 40-bit (10-digit) or 104-bit (26-digit) WEP-PSK passphrases.

ssid.description:
Property to access the decoded SSID of a network.
If this field is set, but HexSSID is not, its value will be UTF-8 encoded and the hex representation will be
assigned to HexSSID.
To configure a non-UTF-8 SSID, field HexSSID must be used.
When reading the configuration of a network, both this field and HexSSID might be set.
Then this field is the decoding of HexSSID.
If possible the HexSSID is decoded using UTF-8, otherwise an encoding is guessed on a best effort basis.

anonymousIdentity.description:
For tunneling protocols only, this indicates the identity of the user presented to the outer protocol.
This value is subject to string expansions. If not specified, use empty string.

clientCertificate.description: Reference to AndroidEnterpriseRelutionCertificate client certificate stored in certificate section.

clientCertificateTemplateUuid.description:
Reference to a certificate template to be used to dynamically generate a client certificate per device, when the policy is applied.

Only one of clientCertificate or `clientCertificateTemplate` should be specified. If both are set, `clientCertificateTemplate` is used.

clientCertificateUsage.description:
Specifies whether the configuration's client certificate or a user based certificate should be used.

If this value is set to USER, the client certificate and template are ignored and the certificate of the user assigned to the device the configuration is applied on is used instead.

domainSuffixMatch.description:
A collection of acceptable domain suffixes.

Values should be valid domain names, e.g. "example.com" or "subdomain.example.com".

Enterprise wireless configurations without this field (or with an empty list as value) are considered insecure and rejected by the platform .

identity.description:
Identity of user.
For tunneling outer protocols (PEAP, EAP-TTLS, and EAP-FAST), this is used to authenticate inside the tunnel, and. 
AnonymousIdentity is used for the EAP identity outside the tunnel.
For non-tunneling outer protocols, this is used for the EAP identity. This value is subject to string expansions.

password.description: Password of user. If not specified, defaults to prompting the user.

serverCAPEMs.description:
Collection of CA certificates in PEM format, used to verify the host's certificate chain.

If this collection contains entries, `serverCARefs` must be empty.

serverCARefs.description:
Collection of CA certificate references, used for verifying the host's certificate chain.

At least one of the CA certificates must match. Cannot be set at the same time as `serverCAPEMs`.

If the collection is empty, the client does not check whether server certificate are signed by a specific CA. A verification using the system’s CA certificates may still apply. See UseSystemCAs for this.

subjectMatch.description: A substring which a remote RADIUS service certificate subject name must contain in order to connect.

subjectAlternativeNameMatch.description:
A list of alternative subject names to be matched against the alternative subject name of an authentication
server certificate.

maxTlsVersion.description:
Sets the maximum TLS protocol version used by the OS for EAP.
This is only needed when connecting to an AP with a buggy TLS implementation, as the protocol will normally
auto-negotiate.

useProactiveKeyCaching.description:
Indicates whether Proactive Key Caching (also known as Opportunistic Key Caching) should be used on a per-service
basis.

excludeDomains.description: Domains and hosts for which to exclude proxy settings.

macAddressRandomizationMode.description:
To set MAC randomization mode, use the property MACAddressRandomizationMode set with the values Hardware or. 
Automatic. This property is currently unavailable in the Open Network Configuration (ONC) specification but is
provided in AMAPI and can be specified while configuring WiFi networks. This applies only to Android 13+ on all
management modes.

value.description: Value of the alternative subject name.

type.description: Type of the alternative subject name.

host.description: Host (or IP address) to use for proxy.

AndroidEnterpriseWorkProfilePasswordConf.description: Specifies the password requirements for a work profile enrolled with Android Enterprise.
