If true, allows the teacher to lock apps or the device without prompting the student. Requires a supervised device.

Version: iOS 11+

Default: false.

forceClassroomUnpromptedScreenObservatio.description:
If true and ScreenObservationPermissionModificationAllowed is also true in the Education payload, a student enrolled in a managed course via the Classroom app automatically gives permission to that course teacher's requests to observe the student's screen without prompting the student. Requires a supervised device.

Version: iOS 11+

Default: false.

forceDelayedSoftwareUpdates.description: If set to true, delays user visibility of software updates.

enforcedSoftwareUpdateDelay.description: Specify a number of days to delay a software update on the device (max. 90 days).

forceAutomaticDateAndTime.description:
If set to true, the Date & Time “Set Automatically” feature is turned on and canʼt be turned off by the user.

Note: The deviceʼs time zone will only be updated when the device can determine its location (cellular connection or wifi with location services enabled).

Version: iOS 12.0

allowPasswordAutoFill.description:
If set to false, users will not be able to use the AutoFill Passwords feature on iOS and will not be prompted to
use
a saved password in Safari or in apps. If set to false, Automatic Strong Passwords will also be disabled and
strong
passwords will not be suggested to users.
Version: iOS 12.0

allowPasswordProximityRequests.description: If set to false, a userʼs device will not request passwords from nearby devices. Defaults to true.

allowPasswordSharing.description: If set to false, users can not share their passwords with the Airdrop Passwords feature.

allowManagedToWriteUnmanagedContacts.description:
If set to true, managed apps can write contacts to unmanaged contacts accounts.

if allowOpenFromManagedToUnmanaged is true, this restriction has no effect.

A payload that sets this to true must be installed via MDM.

allowUnmanagedToReadManagedContacts.description:
If set to true, unmanaged apps can read from managed contacts accounts.

if allowOpenFromManagedToUnmanaged is true, this restriction has no effect.

A payload that sets this to true must be installed via MDM.

allowESIMModification.description: If set to false, the user may not remove or add a cellular plan to the eSIM on the device.

allowSpellCheck.description: If set to false, disables keyboard spell-check.

allowPredictiveKeyboard.description: If set to false, disables predictive keyboards.

allowAutoCorrection.description: If set to false, disables keyboard auto-correction.

allowDictation.description: If set to false, disallows dictation input.

forceWiFiPowerOn.description:
If true, prevents turning off the Wi-Fi in the settings. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 13 and
later.n
Default: false.

allowContinuousPathKeyboard.description:
If false, disables continuous path keyboard. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 13 and later.n
Default: true.

allowFilesNetworkDriveAccess.description: If false, prevents connecting to network drives in the Files app, Available in iOS 13.0 and later.

allowFilesUSBDriveAccess.description: If false, prevents connecting to any connected USB devices in the Files app. Available in iOS 13.0 and later.

allowFindMyDevice.description:
If false, disables Find My Device in the Find My App. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 13 and
later.

allowFindMyFriends.description:
If false, disables Find My Friends in the Find my app. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 13 and
later.

allowSharedDeviceTemporarySession.description: If false, temporary sessions aren’t available on Shared iPad. Available in iOS 13.4 and later.

allowVPNCreation.description: If false, disables the creation of VPN configurations.

allowAppClips.description:
If false, prevents a user from adding any App Clips, and removes any existing App Clips on the device.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 14.0 and later.n
Default: true.

allowApplePersonalizedAdvertising.description:
If false, limits Apple personalized advertising. Available in iOS 14 and later.n
Default: true.

allowAutoUnlock.description:
If false, disallows auto unlock. Available in macOS 10.12 and later, and iOS 14.5 and later.
Default: true.

allowUnpairedExternalBootToRecovery.description:
If true, allows devices to be booted into recovery by an unpaired device.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 14.5 and later.
Default: false.

forceOnDeviceOnlyDictation.description:
If true, disables connections to Siri servers for the purposes of dictation. Available in iOS 14.5 and later.
Default: false.

forceOnDeviceOnlyTranslation.description:
If true, the device won’t connect to Siri servers for the purposes of translation. Available in iOS 15 and later.
Default: false.

requireManagedPasteboard.description:
If true, copy and paste functionality respects the
allowOpenFromManagedToUnmanaged and
allowOpenFromUnmanagedToManaged restrictions.
Default: false.

allowMailPrivacyProtection.description:
If true, it hides your IP address so senders can’t link it to your other online activity or determine your location.
It also prevents senders from seeing if you’ve opened the email they sent you.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 15.1 and later.
Default: false.

allowRapidSecurityResponseInstallation.description:
If set to false, rapid security responses can't be installed.
Default: true.

allowRapidSecurityResponseRemoval.description:
If set to false, rapid security responses can't be removed.
Default: true.

allowiPhoneWidgetsOnMac.description:
If false, disallows iPhone widgets on a Mac that has signed in the same AppleID for iCloud.
n
Available on iOS 17+. Supervised only.

allowMarketplaceAppInstallation.description:
If false, the system prevents installation of alternative marketplace apps from the web and prevents any installed
alternative marketplace apps from installing apps.
n
Available in iOS 17.4 and later.

allowGenmoji.description: If false, prohibits creating new Genmoji. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later.

allowImagePlayground.description:
If false, prohibits the use of image generation. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later and
macOS 15 and later.

allowIPhoneMirroring.description:
If false, prohibits the use of iPhone Mirroring. When used on macOS, this prevents the Mac from mirroring any iPhone.
When used on iOS, this prevents the iPhone from mirroring to any Mac. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS
18 and later and macOS 15 and later.

allowWritingTools.description:
If false, disables Apple Intelligence writing tools. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later and
macOS 15 and later.

allowAutoDim.description:
If false, disables auto dim on iPads with OLED displays.Requires a supervised device in iOS. Available in iOS 17.4
and later.

allowESIMOutgoingTransfers.description:
If false, prevents the transfer of an eSIM from the device on which the restriction is installed to a different
device. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later.

allowImageWand.description: If false, prohibits the use of Image Wand. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later.

allowLiveVoicemail.description:
If false, the system disables live voicemail on the device.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 17.2 and later.

allowPersonalizedHandwritingResults.description:
If false, prevents the system from generating text in the user’s handwriting. Requires a supervised device. Available
in iOS 18 and later.

allowWebDistributionAppInstallation.description: If false, the device prevents installation of apps directly from the web. Available in iOS 17.5 and later.

forcePreserveESIMOnErase.description:
If true, the system preserves eSIM when it erases the device due to too many failed password attempts or the Erase. 
All Content and Settings option in Settings > General > Reset. Available in iOS 17.2 and later.

allowAppsToBeHidden.description:
If set to false, the user is prevented from hiding the app. It does
not affect the user's ability to leave it in the App Library, while removing
it from the home screen. Available in iOS 18.0 and later.

allowAppsToBeLocked.description:
If set to false, the user is prevented from locking the app. Because
hiding an app also requires locking it, disallowing the user from locking
the app will also prevent the user from hiding it. Available in iOS 18.0 and later.

allowMailSummary.description: Available in iOS 18.1 and macOS 15.1 and later.

allowExternalIntelligenceIntegrations.description:
If false, disables the use of external, cloud-based intelligence services
with Siri. Available in iOS 18.2.

allowExternalIntelligenceIntegrationsSig.description:
If false, forces external intelligence providers into anonymous mode. If
a user is already signed in to an external intelligence provider, applying this
restriction will cause them to be signed out. Available in iOS 18.2.

disableJailbreakDetection.description: Whether jailbreak detection should be skipped for the device. If enabled the Relution Agent will always report `false` for the {@link Device#isJailbroken device jailbreak status} . This is managed by the Relution server and is not part of the restriction configuration payload.

allowSafariPrivateBrowsing.description:
If false, the system disables the ability to use private browsing in Safari.
Available in iOS 26 and later.

allowSafariHistoryClearing.description:
If false, the system disables the ability to clear browsing history in Safari.
Available in iOS 26 and later.

allowedCameraRestrictionBundleIDs.description:
If present, the system exempts apps with bundle IDs in the array from the allowCamera restriction. The system doesn’t
grant these apps access to the camera automatically; they’re only exempted from the allowCamera restriction. This key
has no effect when the camera isn’t restricted. Multiple payloads combine using an intersect operation.
n
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 26 and later.

autoDeploymentsDisabled.description:
If `true`, the logged in user's auto deployments will not be performed. This can e.g. be used
for shared devices designated to be used in lesson mode only to speed up user switching.

enforceLoginViaAppLock.description:
If `true`, lock the Relution client until a user has signed in. If `false`, only whitelist the Relution client (Settings is whitelisted by default). Defaults to `true` to stay backwards compatible.

NOTE: When not enforced, the user has to manually open the Relution client to login in order to get further apps whitelisted/installed. However, the whitelist approach has the advantage of always having access to the Settings app. This allows the user to change the WiFi even if not logged in to Relution (e.g. if a student takes a shared device home).

allowLoginViaQrCode.description:
If `true`, the Relution Client will show an option in the login screen to log in via QR code.
The QR code is a AccessToken with. 
SHARED_DEVICE_LOGIN as scope value.

enableScheduledLogout.description: a flag to disable/enable the scheduled logout.

scheduledLogoutDelay.description: the amount of minutes between the last login and the automatic logout.

disableSsoLogin.description:
Whether the login screen on a shared device offers SSO login options. When set, SSO is disallowed on shared devices
so that only username and password is visible.

smimeSigningEnabled.description: If set to `true`, S/MIME signing is enabled for this account.

smimeSigningUserOverrideable.description: If set to `true`, the user can toggle S/MIME signing on or off in Settings.

smimeSigningCertificateUsage.description:
When USER the per-user. 
UserCertificate of subtype
SMIME_SIGNING is used for
mail signatures.

smimeSigningCertificate.description: The identity certificate used to sign messages sent from this account.

smimeSigningCertificateUUIDUserOverridea.description: If set to `true`, the user can select the signing identity.

smimeEncryptByDefault.description:
If set to `true`, S/MIME encryption is enabled by default. If smimeEnablePerMessageSwitch is
`false`, this default cannot be changed by the user.

smimeEnablePerMessageSwitch.description: If `true`, displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI.

smimeEncryptByDefaultUserOverrideable.description: If set to `true`, the user can toggle the encryption by default setting.

smimeEncryptionCertificateUsage.description:
When USER the per-user. 
UserCertificate of subtype
SMIME_ENCRYPTION is used for
mail encryption.

smimeEncryptionCertificate.description:
The identity certificate used to decrypt messages sent to this account.
The public certificate is attached to outgoing mail to allow encrypted mail to be sent to this user.
When the user sends encrypted mail, the public certificate is used to encrypt the copy of the mail in their Sent
mailbox.

smimeEncryptionCertificateUUIDUserOverri.description: If set to `true`, the user can select the S/MIME encryption identity and encryption is enabled.

IosUpdateConfiguration.description: Contains configuration pertaining to the iOS software updates.

imageData.description: Byte array representation of the image to be used for the wallpaper. Image must be either PNG or JPEG format.

lockScreenImageNull.description: Not supported by all configurations. Will return null if not supported.

lockScreenImage.description: Not supported by all configurations. Will return null if not supported.

lockScreenText.description: Not supported by all configurations. Will return null if not supported.

lockScreenTextColor.description: Not supported by all configurations. Will return null if not supported.

captiveBypass.description:
Optional. If set to `true`, Captive Network detection will be
bypassed when the device connects to the network. Defaults
to `false`.

proxyServer.description: The proxy serverʼs network address. Must be provided if proxyType is Manual.

proxyServerPort.description: The proxy serverʼs port. Must be provided if proxyType is Manual.

proxyUsername.description:
Optional. The username used to authenticate to the proxy server. Must be provided if proxyType is. 
Manual.

proxyPassword.description:
Optional. The password used to authenticate to the proxy server. Must be provided if proxyType is. 
Manual.

proxyPACURL.description:
Optional. The URL of the PAC file that defines the proxy Must be provided if proxyType is. 
Manual. configuration.

proxyPACFallbackAllowed.description:
Optional. If false, prevents the device from connecting directly to the destination if the PAC file is unreachable. Default is false.

Must be provided if proxyType is Manual.

sourceSensorReferenceDefaultIfNull.description:
NonNull getter, that returns either the persisted sourceSensorReference,
or if null, the default configuration without changing the entity itself.

targetSensorReferenceDefaultIfNull.description:
NonNull getter, that returns either the persisted targetSensorReference,
or if null, the default configuration without changing the entity itself.

rsyslogConf.description:
configuration file to use for `rsyslogd`.

The content is required to be a valid `rsyslogd` configuration script as defined by https://www.rsyslog.com/doc/v8-stable/index.html.

.

rsyslogDefaultNetstreamDriver.description:
default netstream driver configuration.

The information contained gets applied as global defaults similar to what is specified in
`https://www.rsyslog.com/doc/v8-stable/tutorials/tls_cert_client.html`.

updateDays.description: days of the week to run the update on.

updateTime.description: when to update in local time zone of the mesh.

serverUrl.description: Alternative server URL for Mender update.

updateUrl.description: where to download the update from.

enabledApps.description: Used to define which apps should be visible in Kiosk mode.

enabledAppsConfiguration.description:
Defines visible apps in Kiosk mode. Contains an optional type parameter for filtering out apps for specific
device types.

enableSingleAppMode.description:
Toggle single app mode.

Only one app will be selected and auto-started whenever it is not running.n

appStoreRefreshInterval.description:
The time, in milliseconds, after which the Relution app should refresh its app store information, while in kiosk
mode, when app store apps are allowed.

gridArrangementType.description: Defines the arrangement of the icons in kiosk mode.

gridX.description: Defines how many icons are allowed per grid row.

gridY.description: Defines how many icons are allowed per grid column.

certificateUsage.description:
Specifies whether the configuration's certificate (template) or a user based certificate should be used.

If this value is set to USER, the certificate and/or template are ignored and the certificate of the user assigned to the device the configuration is applied on is used instead.

country.description:
required for Windows, see
{@link WindowsAppstoreService#getRequiredStoreAppProperties(String, String) WindowsAppstoreService#getRequiredStoreAppProperties(String, String)}.

vppLicenseBaseInfo.description: Used only as additional information for portal, value is set before sending the response (serializable only).

LoginItem.description: An item that can be launched automatically.

adminHostInfo.description:
The admin host info. If present in the payload, the system displays its value in the login window as additional
computer information. Before macOS 10.10, this string could only contain host name, system version, or IP address.
After macOS 10.10, setting this key to any value allows the user to click the time area of the menu bar to toggle
through various computer information values.

allowList.description:
The list of user GUIDs or group GUIDs of users that the system allows to log in. An asterisk (*) string specifies all
users or groups.

autologinUsername.description: The user short name to set up auto login.

denyList.description:
The list of user GUIDs or group GUIDs of users that the system disallows to log in. This list takes priority over the
list in the AllowList key.

disableConsoleAccess.description: If true, the system disregards the >console special user name, which provides a command line UI.

disableFDEAutoLogin.description: If true, the system disables the automatic login option when using FileVault.

disableScreenLockImmediate.description: If true, the system disables the immediate Screen Lock functions. Available in macOS 10.13 and later.

hideAdminUsers.description: If true, the system hides administrator users when showing a user list.

hideLocalUsers.description: If true, the system shows only network and system users when showing a user list.

hideMobileAccounts.description: If true, the system hides mobile account users in a user list. In some cases, mobile users show up as network users.

includeNetworkUser.description: If true, the system shows network users when showing a user list.

loginWindowText.description: The text to display in the login window.

logOutDisabledWhileLoggedIn.description: If true, the system disables the Log Out menu item when the user is logged in. Available in macOS 10.13 and later.

powerOffDisabledWhileLoggedIn.description: If true, the system disables the Power Off menu item when the user is logged in.

restartDisabled.description: If true, the system disables the Restart item.

restartDisabledWhileLoggedIn.description: If true, the system disables the Restart menu item when the user is logged in.

showFullname.description: If true, the system shows the name and password dialog. If false, the system displays a list of users.

showOtherUsersManaged.description: If true, the system displays “Other…” when it shows a list of users.

showInputMenu.description: If true, the system shows the Input Menu in the login window.

shutDownDisabled.description: If true, the system disables the Shut Down button.

sleepDisabled.description: If true, the system disables the Sleep button.

shutDownDisabledWhileLoggedIn.description: If true, the system disables the Shut Down menu item when the user is logged in.

disableLoginItemsSuppression.description: If true, the system prevents the user from disabling login item launches by using the Shift key.

loginScripts.description: An array of one or more dictionaries of scripts to run at user login time.

logoutScripts.description: An array of one or more dictionaries of scripts to run at user logout time.

skipLoginHook.description: If true, the system doesn’t execute the login scripts during login.

skipLogoutHook.description: If true, the system doesn’t execute the logout scripts during logout.

closeViewFarPoint.description: The minimum zoom level in the Zoom options.

closeViewHotkeysEnabled.description: If true, enables "Use keyboard shortcuts" in the Zoom options.

closeViewNearPoint.description: The maximum zoom level in the Zoom options.

closeViewScrollWheelToggle.description: If true, enables "Use scroll gesture" in the Zoom options.

closeViewSmoothImages.description: If true, enables "Smooth images" in the Zoom options.

contrast.description:
The contrast value in the Display options.
Minimum Value: 0
Maximum Value: 1.

flashScreen.description: If true, enables "Flash the screen" in the Audio options.

mouseDriver.description: If true, enables Mouse Keys in the Mouse & Trackpad options.

mouseDriverIgnoreTrackpad.description: If true, ignores the built-in trackpad.

mouseDriverInitialDelay.description: The initial delay before moving the mouse with Mouse Keys.

mouseDriverMaxSpeed.description: The maximum speed for the cursor when using Mouse Keys.

slowKey.description: If true, enables "Slow Keys" in the Keyboard options.

slowKeyBeepOn.description: If true, enables "click key sounds" for Slow Keys.

slowKeyDelay.description: The acceptance delay, in milliseconds, for Slow Keys.

stereoAsMono.description: If true, plays stereo audio as mono.

stickyKey.description: If true, enables Sticky Keys in the Keyboard options.

stickyKeyBeepOnModifier.description: If true, enables the beep when a modifier key is set for Sticky Keys.

stickyKeyShowWindow.description: If true, enables "Display pressed keys on screen" for Sticky Keys.

whiteOnBlack.description: If true, enables Invert Colors in Display Accommodations.

disableGuestAccount.description: If true, disables the guest account. This property has no effect if enableGuestAccount is true.

enableGuestAccount.description: If true, enables the guest account.

askForSecureTokenAuthBypass.description: If true, bypasses the secure token authorization dialog. This dialog only appears on APFS volumes.

disusedSeconds.description:
The minimum number of seconds a mobile account can exist before an automatic attempt is made to remove the mobile
account.
Set to 0 to try to remove it at next login or logout time.
Set to -1 to never try to remove the mobile account.

allowNever.description:
If true, allows the user to stop the prompts about mobile account creation every time the user logs in.
This key is only valid if com.apple.cachedaccounts.WarnOnCreate is set to true.

createAtLogin.description: If true, creates the mobile account at login time.

warnOnCreate.description: If true, asks the user if the mobile account should be created and allow the user to not create it.

disableSoftwareUpdateNotifications.description:
If true, disables software update notifications.
macOS 10.10+

restrictStoreDisableAppAdoption.description:
If true, disables app adoption by users.
macOS 10.10+

restrictStoreRequireAdminToInstall.description:
If true, restricts app installations to admin users only.
Deprecated in macOS 10.14.
Use the com.apple.SoftwareUpdate payload key restrict-software-update-require-admin-to-install as a replacement.

restrictStoreSoftwareupdateOnly.description:
If true, restricts app installations to software updates only.
macOS 10.10+

overridePicturePath.description:
The path to the desktop picture. If set, this picture is always locked.
The locked property is replaced by allowWallpaperModification property in macOS 10.13+.

allowMultiDomainAuth.description: If true, the system allows authentication from any domain in the namespace.

allowMultiDomainAuthFlag.description: f true, the system enables the ADAllowMultiDomainAuth key.

createMobileAccountAtLogin.description: If true, the system creates a mobile account at login.

createMobileAccountAtLoginFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADCreateMobileAccountAtLogin key.

defaultUserShellFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADDefaultUserShell key.

domainAdminGroupList.description: The list of Active Directory groups with admin access.

domainAdminGroupListFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADDomainAdminGroupList key.

forceHomeLocal.description: If true, the system forces a local home directory.

forceHomeLocalFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADForceHomeLocal key.

mapGGIDAttribute.description: The map group GID to attribute.

mapGGIDAttributeFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADMapGIDAttribute key.

mapGIDAttributeFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADMapGIDAttribute key.

mapUIDAttributeFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADMapUIDAttribute key.

mountStyle.description: The network home protocol to use: afp or smb.

namespace.description: The primary user account naming convention; either forest or domain.

namespaceFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADNamespace key.

organizationalUnit.description: The organizational unit to add the joining computer object to.

packetEncryptFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADPacketEncrypt key.

packetSignFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADPacketSign key.

preferredDCServerFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADPreferredDCServer key.

restrictDDNS.description: An array of strings that represent the interfaces allowed for dynamic DNS updates, such as en0 and en1.

restrictDDNSFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADRestrictDDNS key.

trustChangePassIntervalDays.description: The number of days before requiring a change of the computer trust account password. Set to 0 to disable the feature.

trustChangePassIntervalDaysFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADTrustChangePassIntervalDays key.

useWindowsUNCPath.description: If true, the system uses the UNC path from Active Directory to derive the network home location.

useWindowsUNCPathFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADUseWindowsUNCPath key.

warnUserBeforeCreatingMA.description: If true, the system enables the warning before creating the mobile account.

warnUserBeforeCreatingMAFlag.description: If true, the system enables the ADWarnUserBeforeCreatingMA key.

hostName.description: (Required) The Active Directory domain to join.

userName.description: The username of the account for the domain.

allowDockFixupOverride.description:
If true, use the file in /Library/Preferences/com.apple.dockfixup.plist when a new user or migrated user logs in.
This option has no effect for existing users.
Available in macOS 10.12 and later. Only available on the device channel.

autohide.description: If true, enables “Automatically hide and show the dock.”

contentsImmutable.description: If true, disables changes to the dock.

dblClickBehavior.description:
The behavior when the window’s title bar is double-clicked.
Possible Values: minimize, maximize, none.

dblClickBehaviorImmutable.description: If true, locks “Double-click a window’s title bar.”

largeSize.description:
The size of the largest magnification.
Minimum: 16
Maximum: 128.

magSizeImmutable.description: If true, locks the magnification slider.

dockSpecialFolders.description:
One or more special folders that may be created at user login time and placed in the dock.
The “My Applications” item is only used for Simple Finder environments.
The “Original Network Home” item is only used for mobile account users.

minEffect.description:
The minimize effect.
Possible Values: genie, scale.

minimizeToApplication.description: If true, enables “Minimize windows into application icon.”

mininToAppImmutable.description: If true, disables the “Minimize windows into application icon” checkbox.

orientation.description:
The orientation of the dock.
Possible Values: bottom, left, right.

persistentApps.description: An array of items located on the Applications side of the Dock that can be removed from the dock.

persistentOthers.description: An array of items located on the Documents side of the Dock that can be removed from the dock.

showProcessIndicators.description: If true, shows the process indicator.

showRecentsImmutable.description: If true, disables “Show recent applications” checkbox.

sizeImmutable.description: If true, locks the size slider.

staticApps.description: An array of items located on the Applications side of the Dock and cannot be removed from that location.

staticOnly.description:
If true, uses the static-apps and static-others dictionaries for the dock and ignores any items in the
persistent-apps and persistent-others dictionaries. If false, the contents are merged with the static items listed
first.

staticOthers.description: An array of items located on the Documents side of the Dock and cannot be removed from that location.

tileSize.description: The tile size. Values must be in the range from 16 to 128.

windowTabbing.description: Set the “Prefer tabs when opening documents” to the provided value.

windowTabbingImmutable.description: If true, disables “Prefer tabs when opening documents” checkbox.

fileType.description:
The type of tile
payload attribute: tile-data.file-type

label.description:
The label of the dock item.
payload attribute: tile-data.label

path.description:
Path to the file, directory or URL link
payload attribute: tile-data.file-data._CFURLString or tile-data.url._CFURLString

pathType.description:
Path type URL or File path
payload attribute: tile-data.file-data._CFURLStringType or tile-data.url._CFURLStringType

displayAs.description:
Display as Folder or Stack,
relevant just for Directory items
payload attribute: tile-data.displayas

homeDirectoryRelative.description:
Home directory relative
payload attribute: tile-data.home directory relative.

showAs.description:
Show as fan, grid or list.
payload attribute: tile-data.showas

MacOSDockConfigurationStaticItem.description: Items that are located on the Documents side of the Dock and cannot be removed from that location.

emailDomains.description:
An array of domains.
Email addresses that lack a suffix matching any of these strings are considered out of domain and marked in Mail.
This is the only field supported on macOS.

desktopSettings.description: The settings for a desktop computer.

desktopSchedule.description: The schedule for turning a computer on and off.

portableAcSettings.description: The settings for a laptop computer using AC power.

portableBatterySettings.description: The settings for a laptop computer using battery power.

destroyFVKeyOnStandby.description: If true, prevents the OS from storing a temporary FileVault key in SMC or RAM for standby.

allowMailDrop.description: If true, this account is allowed to use Mail Drop.

enableRecoveryKeyEscrow.description: The FDE Recovery Key Escrow configuration is represented as an option. If enabled relution server uses a generated certificate.

certificate.description: The DER-encoded certificate data if UseRecoveryKey is true.

defer.description:
If true, the system defers enabling FileVault until the designated user logs out. For details, see fdesetup(8). Only
a local user or a mobile account user can enable FileVault.

deferDontAskAtUserLogout.description: If true, the system prevents requests to enable FileVault at user logout time.

deferForceAtUserLoginMaxBypassAttempts.description:
The maximum number of times users can bypass enabling FileVault before the system requires the user to enable it to
log in. If the value is 0, the system requires the user to enable FileVault the next time they attempt to log in. Set
this key to –1 to disable this feature.

enable.description:
(Required) Set to On to enable FileVault and set to Off to disable FileVault. Payloads set to On sent through MDM
need to either include full authentication information in the payload or have the Defer option set to true. When. 
Defer is true, the system prompts for the authentication information when the user enables FileVault.

outputPath.description: The path to the location of the recovery key and computer information property list.

password.description:
The password of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault. Use the UserEntersMissingInfo key to prompt for this
information.

showRecoveryKey.description: If false, the system prevents display of the personal recovery key to the user after the system enables FileVault.

useKeyChain.description:
If true and you don’t include certificate information in this payload, the system uses the keychain created at
/Library/Keychains/FileVaultMaster.keychain when it adds the institutional recovery key.

useRecoveryKey.description: If true, the system creates a personal recovery key and displays it to the user.

userEntersMissingInfo.description: If true, the system enables a prompt for missing user name or password fields.

username.description: The user name of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault.

MacOSFileVaultConfigurationFdeFileVault.description: Option forceEnableInSetupAssistant is ignored for now.

destroyFVKeyOnStandby.description: If true, the system won’t store the FileVault key across restarts.

dontAllowFDEDisable.description: If true, the system won’t disable FileVault.

dontAllowFDEEnable.description: If true, the system won’t enable FileVault.

prohibitBurn.description: If true, the system disables the Finder’s burn support.

prohibitConnectTo.description: If true, the system disables Connect to Server.

prohibitEject.description: If true, the system disables Eject.

prohibitGoToFolder.description: If true, the system disables Go to Folder.

showExternalHardDrivesOnDesktop.description: If false, the system doesn’t show external hard drives on the Desktop.

showHardDrivesOnDesktop.description: If false, the system doesn’t show internal hard drives on the Desktop.

showMountedServersOnDesktop.description: If false, the system doesn’t show mounted file servers on the Desktop.

showRemovableMediaOnDesktop.description: If false, the system doesn’t show removable media items on the Desktop.

warnOnEmptyTrash.description: If false, the system doesn’t warn the user before emptying the trash.

allowSigned.description:
If true, the system allows built-in software to receive incoming connections.
Available in macOS 12.3 and later.

allowSignedApp.description:
If true, the system allows downloaded signed software to receive incoming connections.
Available in macOS 12.3 and later.

blockAllIncoming.description: If true, the system enables blocking all incoming connections.

enableFirewall.description: (Required) If true, the system enables the firewall.

enableStealthMode.description: If true, the system enables stealth mode.

applications.description: The list of apps with connections that the firewall controls.

allowed.description: If true, the system allows connections for the app.

bundleID.description: The bundle identifier for the app.

MacOSFirewallConfigurationApplicationIte.description: A dictionary of details for apps.

autoLogOutDelay.description:
The auto log out delay (in seconds).
A value of zero, means auto-logout is off. In some cases, this value may be restricted to values between 5
minutes and 24 hours.

multipleSessionEnabled.description: If set to false, fast user switching is disabled.

allowedKernelExtensions.description:
The dictionary that represents a set of kernel extensions that the system always allows to load on the computer. The
dictionary maps team identifiers (keys) to arrays of bundle identifiers.

allowNonAdminUserApprovals.description:
If true, nonadministrative users can approve additional kernel extensions in the Security & Privacy preferences.
Available in macOS 11 and later.

allowedTeamIdentifiers.description: The array of team identifiers that define which validly signed kernel extensions can load.

allowUserOverrides.description: If true, users can approve additional kernel extensions that configuration profiles don’t explicitly allow.

exceptionsList.description: The list of hosts and domains that should bypass proxy settings.

ftpPassive.description: If true, enables passive FTP mode.

ftpProxy.description: The host name or IP address for the FTP proxy.

gopherProxy.description: The host name or IP address for the gopher proxy.

httpProxy.description: The host name or IP address for the web proxy.

httpsEnable.description: If true, enables secure web proxy.

httpsProxy.description: The host name or IP address for the secure web proxy.

proxyAutoConfigEnable.description: If true, enables automatic proxy configuration.

rtspProxy.description: The host name or IP address for the streaming proxy.

socksEnable.description: If true, enable the SOCKS proxy.

socksProxy.description: The host name or IP address for the SOCKS proxy.

fallBackAllowed.description:
If 1, enables cellular fallback. Default is 1.
For managed devices, if not supplied, the default is 0.

contentFilter.description: The payload you use to configure the parental control web content filters.

dictionary.description: The payload you use to configure parental control dictionary restrictions.

timeLimits.description: The payload you use to configure parental control time limits.

gameCenter.description: The payload you use to configure Game Center parental controls.

appRestrictions.description: The payload you use to configure parental controls for apps.

allowSafariSummary.description:
If false, the system disables the ability to summarize content in Safari.
Available in macOS 15.2 and later.

allowMailSmartReplies.description:
If false, disables smart replies in Mail.
Available in macOS 15.2 and later.

allowAppleIntelligenceReport.description:
If false, the system disables Apple Intelligence reports.
Available in macOS 15.4.

ratingTVShows.description:
The maximum level of TV content allowed on the device.
Available in macOS 15 and later.
Possible values, with the US description of the rating level:
n
1000: All
600: TV-MA
500: TV-14
400: TV-PG
300: TV-G
200: TV-Y7
100: TV-Y.

ratingMovies.description:
The maximum level of movie content allowed on the device.
Available in macOS 15 and later.
Possible values, with the US description of the rating level:
n
1000: All
500: NC-17
400: R
300: PG-13
200: PG
100: G
0: None.

ratingApps.description:
The maximum level of app content allowed on the device.
Preinstalled (first party) apps ignore this restriction. 
Available in macOS 15 and later.
Possible values, with the US description of the rating level:
n
1000: All
600: 17+
300: 12+
200: 9+
100: 4+
0: None.

forceBypassScreenCaptureAlert.description:
If true, then the system bypasses the presentation of a screen capture alert.
Available in macOS 15.1 and later.

allowExplicitContent.description:
If false, the system hides explicit music or video content purchased from the iTunes Store.
The system marks explicit content as such by content providers, such as record labels, when sold through the iTunes. 
Store.
Explicit content in the News and Podcast apps is also hidden.
Available in macOS 15 and later.

allowBookstoreErotica.description:
If false, the system prevents the user from downloading Apple Books media that’s tagged as erotica.
Available in macOS 15 and later.

allowBookstore.description:
If false, the system removes the Book Store tab from the Books app.
Available in macOS 15 and later.

allowNotesTranscription.description:
If false, disables transcription in Notes.
Available in macOS 15.2 and later.

allowNotesTranscriptionSummary.description:
If false, disables transcription summarization in Notes.
Available in macOS 15.3 and later.

allowedExternalIntelligenceWorkspaceIDs.description:
An array of strings, but currently restricted to a single element.
If present, Apple Intelligence only allows the given external integration workspace ID to be used, and requires a
sign-in to make requests.
The user will be required to sign in to integrations that support signing in.
Multiple payloads combine using an intersect operation.
This means the allowed set of workspace IDs can become the empty set if conflicting values are specified in multiple
payloads.
macOS 15.3 or later.

allowPasswordAutoFill.description:
If set to false, users will not be able to use the AutoFill Passwords feature on iOS and will not be prompted to use
a saved password in Safari or in apps. If set to false, Automatic Strong Passwords will also be disabled and strong
passwords will not be suggested to users.
Version: iOS 12.0

allowPasswordProximityRequests.description: If set to false, a userʼs device will not request passwords from nearby devices. Defaults to true.

allowPasswordSharing.description: If set to false, users can not share their passwords with the Airdrop Passwords feature.

allowAirDrop.description:
If set to false, AirDrop is disabled. Available in iOS 7 and later, and macOS 10.13 and later.n
Default: true.

allowAddingGameCenterFriends.description:
If false, prohibits adding friends to Game Center.
As of iOS 13, requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 4.2.1 and later, and macOS 10.13 and later.n
Default: true.

allowDiagnosticSubmission.description:
If false, prevents the device from automatically submitting diagnostic reports to Apple.
Available in iOS 6 and later, and macOS 10.13 and later. Also available for user enrollment.n
Default: true.

allowDictation.description:
If false, disallows dictation input.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 10.3 and later, and macOS 10.13 and later.n
Default: true.

allowPasscodeModification.description:
If false, prevents the device passcode from being added, changed, or removed.
This restriction is ignored by Shared iPads. Requires a supervised device.
Available in iOS 9 and later, and macOS 10.13 and later.n
Default: true.

allowRemoteScreenObservation.description:
If false, disables remote screen observation by the Classroom app.
Nest this key beneath allowScreenShot as a subrestriction.
If allowScreenShot is set to false, the Classroom app doesn't observe remote screens.
Required a supervised device until iOS 13 and macOS 10.15.
Available in iOS 12 and later, and macOS 10.14.4 and later.n
Default: true.

allowScreenShot.description:
If false, disables saving a screenshot of the display and capturing a screen recording.
It also disables the Classroom app from observing remote screens.
Available in iOS 4 and later, and macOS 10.14.4 and later. Also available for user enrollment.n
Default: true.

allowWallpaperModification.description:
If false, prevents wallpaper from being changed.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 9 and later, and macOS 10.13 and later.n
Default: true.

enforcedSoftwareUpdateDelay.description:
Sets how many days to delay a software update on the device.
With this restriction in place, the user doesn't see a software update until the specified number of days after
the software update release date.
This value is used by forceDelayedAppSoftwareUpdates and forceDelayedSoftwareUpdates.
Requires a supervised device in iOS and tvOS.
Available in iOS 11.3 and later, macOS 10.13.4 and later, and tvOS 12.2 and later.n
Default: 30
Minimum: 1
Maximum: 90.

forceClassroomAutomaticallyJoinClasses.description:
If true, automatically gives permission to the teacher's requests without prompting the student.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 11 and later, and macOS 10.14.4 and later.n
Default: false.

forceClassroomRequestPermissionToLeaveCl.description:
If true, a student enrolled in an unmanaged course through Classroom requests permission from the teacher when
attempting to leave the course.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 11.3 and later, and macOS 10.14.4 and later.n
Default: false.

forceClassroomUnpromptedAppAndDeviceLock.description:
If true, allows the teacher to lock apps or the device without prompting the student.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 11 and later, and macOS 10.14.4 and later.n
Default: false.

forceDelayedAppSoftwareUpdates.description:
If true, delays user visibility of non-OS Software Updates. Requires a supervised device.
Visibility of Operating System updates is controlled through forceDelayedSoftwareUpdates.
The delay is 30 days unless enforcedSoftwareUpdateDelay is set to another value.
Available in macOS 11 and later.n
Default: false.

forceDelayedSoftwareUpdates.description:
If true, delays user visibility of software updates. In macOS, seed build updates are allowed, without delay.
Requires a supervised device in iOS and tvOS.
The delay is 30 days unless enforcedSoftwareUpdateDelay is set to another value.
Available in iOS 11.3 and later, macOS 10.13 and later, and tvOS 12.2 and later.n
Default: false.

allowUIConfigurationProfileInstallation.description:
If 'false', prohibits the user from installing configuration profiles and
certificates interactively. Requires a supervised device.
Available in iOS 6 and later and macOS 13 and later.
Default: true.

allowUSBRestrictedMode.description:
If 'false', allows the device to always connect to USB accessories while locked.
On macOS, allows new USB accessories to connect without authorization.
This value is ignored if Lockdown mode is enabled. Requires a supervised device.
Available in iOS 11.4.1 and later and macOS 13 and later.
Default: true.

allowUniversalControl.description:
If 'false', disables Universal Control. Available in macOS 13 and later.
Default: true.

allowRapidSecurityResponseInstallation.description:
If set to false, rapid security responses can't be installed.
Default: true.

allowRapidSecurityResponseRemoval.description:
If set to false, rapid security responses can't be removed.
Default: true.

allowAirPlayIncomingRequests.description:
If false, disables incoming AirPlay requests.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in macOS 12.3 and later, and tvOS 10.2 and later.

allowApplePersonalizedAdvertising.description:
If false, limits Apple personalized advertising.
n
Available in iOS 14 and later and macOS 12 and later.

allowBluetoothModification.description:
If false, prevents modification of Bluetooth settings.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 11 and later.

allowCloudPrivateRelay.description:
If false, disables iCloud Private Relay.
n
For iOS devices, this restriction requires a supervised device.
n
Available in macOS 12 and later, and iOS 15 and later.

allowDefinitionLookup.description:
If false, disables definition lookup.
n
Requires a supervised device on iOS.
n
Available in iOS 8.1.3 and later and macOS 10.11 and later.

allowEraseContentAndSettings.description:
If false, disables the Erase All Content And Settings option in the Reset UI.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 8 and later, and macOS 12 and later.

allowFindMyDevice.description:
If false, disables Find My Device in the Find My app.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 13 and later.

allowFindMyFriends.description:
If false, disables Find My Friends in the Find My app.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 13 and later.

enforcedFingerprintTimeout.description:
The value, in seconds, after which the fingerprint unlock requires a password to authenticate.
The default value is 48 hours.
n
Available in macOS 12 and later.

enforcedSoftwareUpdateMajorOSDeferredIns.description:
This restriction allows the admin to set how many days to delay a major software upgrade on the device. When this
restriction is in place the user sees a software upgrade only after the specified delay after the release of the
software upgrade. This value controls the delay for forceDelayedMajorSoftwareUpdates.
n
Available in macOS 11.3 and later.

enforcedSoftwareUpdateMinorOSDeferredIns.description:
This restriction allows the admin to set how many days to delay a minor OS software update on the device. When this
restriction is in place the user see a software update only after the specified delay after the release of the
software update. This value controls the delay for forceDelayedSoftwareUpdates.

enforcedSoftwareUpdateNonOSDeferredInsta.description:
This restriction allows the admin to set how many days to delay an app software update on the device. When this
restriction is in place the user sees a non-OS software update only after the specified delay after the release of
the software. This value controls the delay for forceDelayedAppSoftwareUpdates.
n
Available in macOS 11.3 and later.

forceAssistantProfanityFilter.description:
If true, forces the use of the profanity filter assistant.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 11 and later and macOS 10.13 and later.

forceClassroomUnpromptedScreenObservatio.description:
If true and ScreenObservationPermissionModificationAllowed is also true in the Education payload, a student enrolled
in a managed course via the Classroom app automatically gives permission to that course teacher’s requests to observe
the student’s screen without prompting the student.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 11 and later, and macOS 10.14.4 and later.

forceDelayedMajorSoftwareUpdates.description:
If set to true, delays user visibility of major upgrades to OS Software.
n
Available in macOS 11.3 and later.

safariAllowAutoFill.description:
If false, disables Safari AutoFill for passwords, contact info, and credit cards and also prevents the Keychain from
being used for AutoFill. Though third-party password managers are allowed and apps can use AutoFill.
n
As of iOS 13, requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 4 and later, and macOS 10.13 and later.

allowAccountModification.description:
If false, disables account modification.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 7 and later, macOS 14 and later, and watchOS 10 and later.

allowARDRemoteManagementModification.description:
If false, prevents modifying the Remote Management Sharing setting in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowAssistant.description:
If false, disables Siri.
n
Available in iOS 5 and later and macOS 14 and later.
n
Also available for user enrollment.

allowBluetoothSharingModification.description:
If false, prevents modifying Bluetooth setting in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowCloudFreeform.description:
If false, disallows iCloud Freeform services.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowDeviceNameModification.description:
If false, prevents the user from changing the device name.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 9 and later, macOS 14 and later, and tvOS 11.0 and later.

allowFileSharingModification.description:
If false, prevents modifying File Sharing setting in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowFingerprintModification.description:
If false, prevents the user from modifying Touch ID or Face ID.
n
Requires a supervised device.
n
Available in iOS 8.3 and later, and macOS 14 and later.

allowTimeMachineBackup.description:
If false, prevents modification of Time Machine settings in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowStartupDiskModification.description:
If false, prevents modification of Startup Disk setting in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowRemoteAppleEventsModification.description:
If false, prevents modifying Remote Apple Events Sharing setting in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowPrinterSharingModification.description:
If false, prevents modifying Printer Sharing setting in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowLocalUserCreation.description:
If false, prevents creating new users in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowInternetSharingModification.description:
If false, prevents modifying Internet Sharing setting in System Settings.
n
Available in macOS 14 and later.

allowGenmoji.description: If false, prohibits creating new Genmoji. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later.

allowImagePlayground.description:
If false, prohibits the use of image generation. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later and
macOS 15 and later.

allowIPhoneMirroring.description:
If false, prohibits the use of iPhone Mirroring. When used on macOS, this prevents the Mac from mirroring any iPhone.
When used on iOS, this prevents the iPhone from mirroring to any Mac. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS
18 and later and macOS 15 and later.

allowWritingTools.description:
If false, disables Apple Intelligence writing tools. Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 18 and later and
macOS 15 and later.

forceOnDeviceOnlyDictation.description:
If true, the system disables connections to Siri servers for the purposes of dictation. Available in iOS 14.5 and
later, macOS 14 and later, and watchOS 10 and later. Also available for user enrollment.

allowMailSummary.description: Available in iOS 18.1 and macOS 15.1 and later.

allowExternalIntelligenceIntegrations.description:
If false, disables the use of external, cloud-based intelligence services
with Siri. Available in macOS 15.2

allowExternalIntelligenceIntegrationsSig.description:
If false, forces external intelligence providers into anonymous mode. If
a user is already signed in to an external intelligence provider, applying this
restriction will cause them to be signed out. Available in macOS 15.2

allowSafariPrivateBrowsing.description:
If false, the system disables the ability to use private browsing in Safari.
Available in macOS 26 and later.

allowSafariHistoryClearing.description:
If false, the system disables the ability to clear browsing history in Safari.
Available in macOS 26 and later.

allowCallRecording.description: If false, disables call recording. Available in iOS 18 and later and macOS 26 and later.

allowLiveVoicemail.description:
If false, the system disables live voicemail on the device.
Requires a supervised device. Available in iOS 17.2 and later and macOS 26 and later.

askForPassword.description:
If `true`, the user is prompted for a password when the screen saver is unlocked or stopped.
When you use this prompt, you must also provide `askForPasswordDelay`.
macOS 10.13+.

askForPasswordDelay.description:
The number of seconds to delay before the password will be required to unlock or stop the screen saver (the
grace period).
A value of 2147483647 (for example, 0x7FFFFFFF) disables this requirement, and on 10.13, the payload is one
of the only ways of disabling the feature.
To use this option, you must set `askForPassword` to `true`.
macOS 10.13+.

loginWindowIdleTime.description:
The number of seconds of inactivity before the screen saver activates (0 = Never activate).
macOS 10.11+.

loginWindowModulePath.description:
The full path to the screen-saver module to use.
macOS 10.11+.

eventTriggersEnabled.description: Enables the use of event based triggers.

eventTriggers.description: List of events to trigger the script execution.

scheduledTriggerEnabled.description: Enables the use of a time based trigger.

scheduledDays.description: List of days on which the script execution is triggered.

scheduledStartTime.description: The start time when the script execution is triggered on the specified days.

timeZone.description: The zone of the start time.

dontAllowFireWallUI.description: If true, disables user changes to the firewall settings.

dontAllowLockMessageUI.description: If true, disables user changes to the lock message.

dontAllowPasswordResetUI.description: If true, disables user changes to the password.

skipAccessibility.description: If true, the system skips the Accessibility pane.

skipAppearance.description: If true, skips the Choose Your Look window.

skipCloudSetup.description: If true, skips the Apple ID setup window.

skipiCloudStorageSetup.description: If true, skips the iCloud Storage window.

skipPrivacySetup.description: If true, skips the Privacy consent window.

skipSiriSetup.description: If true, skips the Siri setup window.

skipTrueTone.description: If true, skips the True Tone Display window.

skipScreenTime.description: If true, skips the Screen Time window.

skipSetupItems.description: An array strings that describe the setup items to skip. SkipKeys provides a list of valid strings and their meanings. Available in iOS 14 and later.

skipTouchIDSetup.description: If true, skips the Touch ID setup window.

skipUnlockWithWatch.description: If true, the system skips the Unlock With Apple Watch pane.

allowedSystemExtensions.description:
A dictionary of approved system extensions on the computer. The dictionary maps the team identifiers (keys) to arrays
of bundle identifiers, where the bundle identifier defines the system extension to install.

allowedSystemExtensionTypes.description:
A dictionary that maps a team identifier to an array of strings, where each string is a type of system extension that
you can install for that team identifier. The allowed extension types are DriverExtension, NetworkExtension, and. 
EndpointSecurityExtension.

allowedTeamIdentifiers.description:
An array of team identifiers that defines valid, signed system extensions that are allowable to load. Approved system
extensions are those signed with any of the specified team identifiers.

allowUserOverrides.description:
If false, restricts users from approving additional system extensions that configuration profiles don’t explicitly
allow.

nonRemovableFromUISystemExtensions.description:
A dictionary of system extensions on the computer. The dictionary maps the team identifiers (keys) to arrays of
bundle identifiers, where the bundle identifier defines the system extension which can’t be disabled or uninstalled
from System Settings or Finder.

nonRemovableSystemExtensions.description:
A dictionary of system extensions on the computer. The dictionary maps the team identifiers (keys) to arrays of
bundle identifiers, where the bundle identifier defines the system extension which can’t be disabled or uninstalled
when SIP is enabled.

removableSystemExtensions.description:
A dictionary of system extensions that are allowed to remove themselves from the machine. The dictionary maps team
identifiers (keys) to arrays of bundle identifiers, where the bundle identifier defines the system extension. An
application using the OSSystemExtensionDeactivationRequest API can deactivate the specified system extensions without
requiring an administrator to authorize the operation.
Available in macOS 12 and later.

allowIdentifiedDevelopers.description:
If `true`, enables Gatekeeper's "Mac App Store and identified developers" option,
otherwise enables Gatekeeper's "Mac App Store".
This has no effect if enableAssessment is not set to `true`.

selectionMode.description: Whether the selected preference panes should be enabled or disabled.

systemPreferencePanes.description: The list of System Preferences panes. Depending on the selectionMode they will be enabled or disabled.

thirdPartyPreferencePanes.description: Bundle ids of third party preference panes. Depending on the selectionMode they will be enabled or disabled.

autoBackup.description: If true, performs automatic backups at regular intervals.

backupAllVolumes.description: If true, backs up only the startup volume by default.

backupDestUrl.description: (Required) The URL of the backup destination.

backupSizeMB.description: The backup size limit, in megabytes. Set to 0 for unlimited.

backupSkipSys.description: If true, skips system files and folders by default.

basePaths.description: The list of paths to back up besides the startup volume.

mobileBackups.description: If true, create local backup snapshots when not connected to the network.

skipPaths.description: The path to skip from start volume.

MacOSUpdateConfiguration.description: Contains configuration pertaining to the iOS software updates.

name.description: Name of the current bookmark folder.

entries.description: Configuration of child level bookmark folders.

name.description: Name of the bookmark.

bookmarks.description: Top level configuration of the bookmark folder.

toplevelName.description: The name of the folder in which Managed Bookmarks live. Defaults to "Managed Bookmarks".

ManagedConfigurationArray.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type bundle array. A bundle array can contain one or more managed configuration bundles.

JSON value: array of objects.

ManagedConfigurationBool.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type bool.

JSON value: `true` or `false`.

ManagedConfigurationBundle.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type bundle. A bundle can contain named configuration values of managed configuration types.

JSON value: object.

ManagedConfigurationChoice.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type choice.

JSON value: string.

ManagedConfigurationHidden.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type hidden.

JSON value: string.

ManagedConfigurationInteger.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type bool.

JSON value: number.

ManagedConfigurationMultiselect.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type bool.

JSON value: array of strings.

ManagedConfigurationString.description:
An app's managed configuration value of type bool.

JSON value: string.

ManagedConfigurationType.description:
Abstract base class for the types of managed configuration values that can be configured for an app.

The format for the configuration is dictated by the. ManagedProperty values supported by the app. Each field name in the managed configuration must match the key field of the `ManagedProperty`. The field value must be compatible with the type of the `ManagedProperty`.

autoLaunchedApplications.description: A list of items to be launched automatically.

requiredCertificates.description: Common names of certificates which should be installed.

violatedCertificates.description: Common names of certificates which are missing.

preconfiguredFiles.description: A list of configurations for Network file shares.

shareUrl.description: The URL of the file or resource to be shared. .

familyControlsEnabled.description: If true, enables app access restrictions.

whiteList.description: The allow list of app item dictionaries.

ParentalControlsApplicationRestrictions.description: The payload you use to configure the parental control app restrictions.

restrictWeb.description: If true, enables web content filters.

whiteListEnabled.description: If true, enables web content filters.

filterBlackList.description:
The array of URLs that defines a deny list. When restrictWeb and useContentFilter are enabled, no URLs in the deny
list are available to the user.

filterWhiteList.description:
The array of URLs that defines an allow list. When restrictWeb and useContentFilter are enabled, only URLs in the
allow list are available to the user.

siteWhiteList.description:
An array of sites that defines an allow list. If specified, this defines additional allowed sites besides those in
the automated allow list and deny list, including disallowed adult sites.
This key is required if whiteListEnabled is true.

ParentalControlsContentFilter.description: The payload you use to configure the parental control web content filters.

parentalControl.description: If true, enables parental controls dictionary restrictions.

ParentalControlsDictionary.description: The payload you use to configure parental control dictionary restrictions.

ParentalControlsGameCenter.description: The payload you use to configure Game Center parental controls.

ParentalControlsTimeLimits.description: The payload you use to configure parental control time limits.

PersistentPreferredActivity.description:
A default activity for handling intents that match a particular intent filter.
Note: To set up a kiosk, use InstallType to KIOSK rather than use persistent preferred activities.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

PowerDefinitionCharacteristic.description:
level and power samples making up a power characteristic of the sensor level value to power consumption.

The arrays must be of same length, the shorter is taken. Levels should be sorted ascending. Actual power
consumption is determined by interpolating the neighboring sensor level values linearly. Sensor level values are
clamped to the range of samples given.

PowerDefinitionSensor.description:
single sensor to deduce the level value from.

The level value is computed by taking the current sensor value and optionally scaling it.

weekdays.description:
Day: 1 -> Sunday, 2 -> Monday, 4 -> Tuesday, 8 -> Wednesday,
16 -> Thursday, 32 -> Friday, 64 -> Saturday, 255 -> Everyday.

bpjmList.description:
The BPjM Youth Protection List is a German index of media considered harmful to minors,
including violent, explicit, or discriminatory content. Media on this list face strict restrictions on advertising,
distribution, and public access to protect children and adolescents.

autoAcceptDataDisclosure.description: Android Enterprise only. Auto accept data disclosure dialog on behalf of user.

extensionType.description: Get iOS File Provider extension type.

subject.description:
The representation of an X.500 name as an array of OID and value.
For example, /C=US/O=Apple Inc. translates to [ [ ["C", "US"] ], [ ["O", "Apple Inc."] ] ].

subjectAlternativeNames.description:
The SCEP payload can specify an optional SubjectAltName dictionary that provides values required by the CA for
issuing a certificate. You can specify a single string or an array of strings for each key.

retries.description: The number of times the device should retry if the server sends a PENDING response.

retryDelay.description: The number of seconds to wait between subsequent retries. The first retry is attempted without this delay.

fingerprint.description: The fingerprint of the Certificate Authority certificate.

allowAllAppsAccess.description: If true, all apps have access to the private key. Default: false.

keyIsExtractable.description: If false, the system disables exporting the private key from the keychain. Default: true.

uuid.description: SCEP configuration UUID.

ScepPolicyRepresentation.description:
Used as a reference to the SCEP Configuration in WifiConfiguration as an
alternative to using one of the uploaded certificates. 
We can not represent this as a Certificate as with SCEP the certificate is
generated on the device. Therefore, we can't store it in the backend DB.

Schedule.description:
DTO for schedules.
n
Extend if required, but other fields than scheduledDays and scheduledStartTime may
be nullable.

inactiveValue.description:
sensor value published when thing is detected as inactive or malfunctioning. The system enters inactivity state for a sensor if
  • no updated value was received for a sensor for several hours,
  • the device is detected as malfunctioning somehow,
  • in the event it is known to power off e.g. for maintenance, or
  • as a final sensor value immediately before withdrawal.
Setting the property to any value (incl. `null`) results in the system to record that value for inactivity. An omitted (that is undefined by default) value disables publication of an inactive value for that sensor.

SensorReference.description: reference to a sensor mapped by type and index.

webOrigin.description: A list of web origins, each with its allowed USB devices.

url.description:
Web origin URL. Access to the devices applies to the entire web origin. Any path will be truncated. For detailed
information on valid URL patterns, see https://cloud.google.com/docs/chrome-enterprise/policies/url-patterns. Note
that using the "*" wildcard is not valid. The maximum size is 1000 characters.

device.description:
A list of USB devices allowed for this web origin. Each device has format 'vendor_id:product_id' where each
of the IDs is a hexadecimal 4-char string. An example of a device ID: '1234:ab12'. The maximum size is 500000
characters (for all entries combined).

temporarySessionOnly.description:
If `true`, the user only sees the Guest Welcome pane and can only log in as a guest user.
If `false`, the user can sign in with a managed Apple ID (the existing behavior).
Available in iOS 14.5+

temporarySessionTimeout.description:
The timeout, in seconds, for the temporary session.
The temporary session logs out automatically after the specified period of inactivity.
The minimum value is 30 seconds.
Setting this value to 0 removes the timeout.
Available in iOS 14.5+

userSessionTimeout.description:
The timeout, in seconds, for the user session.
The user session logs out automatically after the specified period of inactivity.
The minimum value is 30 seconds.
Setting this value to 0 removes the timeout.
Available in iOS 14.5 and later.

quotaSize.description:
The quota size, in megabytes (MB), for each user on the shared device, or if the quota size is too small, the minimum
quota size.
If a value for quotaSize and
residentUsers is provided,
the value of quotaSize is used.

residentUsers.description:
The expected number of users.
If this value is greater than the value for the maximum possible number of users that the device supports, the MDM
server uses maximum possible number instead.

courses.description:
Ids of courses which should be available to login from the sharedIpad. 
IosEducationConfiguration.

blockRelutionUserChange.description:
A Relution flag, which will block the automatic relution user assignment.
When true, will always leave the assigned user as is.

SiteWhiteListItem.description: A dictionary defining a site for the allow list.

TimeLimit.description: Time limit DTO for MacOS Parental Controls time limits configuration.

blacklistedAppBundleIDs.description:
If present, the system prevents showing or launching apps with bundle IDs in the array.
Include the value com.apple.webapp to restrict all webclips.
Requires a supervised device. 
Available in iOS 15 and later, and tvOS 15 and later.

whitelistedAppBundleIDs.description:
If present, the system only shows or can launch apps with bundle IDs in the array.
Include the value com.apple.webapp to allow all webclips.
Requires a supervised device.
Available in iOS 15 and later, and tvOS 15 and later.

allowPasswordProximityRequests.description: If set to false, a userʼs device will not request passwords from nearby devices. Defaults to true.

enforcedSoftwareUpdateDelay.description: Specify a number of days to delay a software update on the device (max. 90 days).

forceAutomaticDateAndTime.description:
If set to true, the Date & Time “Set Automatically” feature is turned on and canʼt be turned off by the user.

Note: The deviceʼs time zone will only be updated when the device can determine its location (cellular connection or wifi with location services enabled).

Version: TvOS 12.2+

forceDelayedSoftwareUpdates.description: If set to true, delays user visibility of software updates.

allowAutomaticScreenSaver.description:
If false, the system disables Apple TV’s automatic screen saver.
Available in tvOS 15.4 and later.
Default: true.

allowCamera.description:
If false, the system disables the camera and removes its icon from the Home screen, and users are unable to take
photographs.
Available in iOS 4 and later, macOS 10.11 and later, and tvOS 17 and later.
Support for this restriction on unsupervised devices is deprecated.
Default: true.

forceAirPlayIncomingRequestsPairingPassw.description:
If true, the system forces all devices sending AirPlay requests to this device to use a pairing password.
Available in tvOS 6.2 and later.
This key isn’t supported in tvOS 10.2 and later.
Use the AirPlay Security Payload instead.
Default: false.

captiveBypass.description:
Optional. If set to `true`, Captive Network detection will be
bypassed when the device connects to the network. Defaults
to `false`.

payloadCertificateUUID.description: certificate payload uuid within the same profile to use for the client credential.

proxyPACFallbackAllowed.description:
Optional. If false, prevents the device from connecting directly
to the destination if the PAC file is unreachable. Default is false.
Must be provided if proxyType is. 
Manual.

proxyPACURL.description:
Optional. The URL of the PAC file that defines the proxy Must be provided if proxyType is. 
Manual.
configuration.

proxyPassword.description:
Optional. The password used to authenticate to the proxy server. Must be provided if proxyType is. 
Manual.

proxyServer.description: The proxy serverʼs network address. Must be provided if proxyType is Manual.

proxyServerPort.description: The proxy serverʼs port. Must be provided if proxyType is Manual.

proxyUsername.description:
Optional. The username used to authenticate to the proxy server. Must be provided if proxyType is. 
Manual.

connectorNumber.description:
Returns the number of the monitor's source port.

If the monitor only has a single port with the associated connector type, the number is `null`.

VideoInputSource.description:
Identifies a monitor's input source.

An input source has a connector type and an optional number, in case the device has multiple such inputs.

Monitors include computer monitors, television sets (TVs) or interactive whiteboards (aka ActivBoard or Smartboard).

tunnelConfigurations.description: When unset or empty, a single default IKEv2 tunnel is applied to all interfaces.

adamId.description: The app's external name (iTunes Store Identifier).

assetsPerToken.description:
Number of licenses in each known VppLicenseStatus per vpp token.
  • key: vpp token uuid
  • value: licenseCountMap for the vpp token
.

VppAssetBaseInfo.description: Represents information about a VPP asset (i.e. a public app or a book from Apple iTunes).

url.description:
Web application origin URL. Access to the devices applies to the entire web origin. Any path will be truncated. For
detailed information on valid URL patterns, see
https://cloud.google.com/docs/chrome-enterprise/policies/url-patterns. Note that using the "*" wildcard is
not valid. The maximum size is 1000 characters.

devices.description:
A list of USB devices allowed for this web application. Each device has format 'vendor_id:product_id' where
each of the IDs is a hexadecimal 4-char string. An example of a device ID: '1234:ab12'. The maximum size is
500000 characters (for all entries combined).

webApplications.description: A list of web applications, each with its allowed USB devices.

.

.

.

.

Domains with no proxy. Sets the domains with no proxy in manual proxy configuration.

.

Autoconfiguration URL. Sets the autoconfiguration URL in automatic proxy configuration.
