.

wifiRoamingSettings.description: Wi-Fi roaming settings. SSIDs provided in this list must be unique, the policy will be rejected otherwise.

wifiRoamingMode.description: Wi-Fi roaming mode for the specified SSID.

fileBaseInfo.description: The base information of the binary file.

fileBytes.description: Base64 encoded content of the configuration file to be sent via SyncML.

appControlId.description:
The unique identifier for the configuration on the device.

It must be contained in the xml file as `` and send as id value for each SyncML request.

certificateTemplateForIdentityCertificat.description:
References a CertificateTemplate. If the configuration gets applied on a
device, this reference will be resolved and the certificate above will be filled
by an issued certificate of this CertificateTemplate.

pkcs12KeyExportable.description:
Flag used for PKCS12 certificates if a user can export the certificate.
PKCS12 is only exportable if pkcs12KeyLocation is. 
SOFTWARE.

logLevel.description:
Defines the log level used by the Windows Companion.

Default is Information.

actionTimeout.description:
Defines the maximum time in minutes for an action to be processed until it is aborted by the Windows Companion.
Default is 45 minutes.

appInstallationTimeout.description:
Defines the maximum time in minutes for an app installation to be processed until it is aborted by the. Windows Companion. Default is 45 minutes.

If not configured the getAppInstallationTimeOut() is used, which is set by setAppInstallationTimeOut(Duration).

appRemovalTimeout.description:
Defines the maximum time in minutes for an app uninstallation to be processed until it is aborted by the. 
Windows Companion. Default is 45 minutes.

pollInterval.description:
Defines the recurring interval in minutes when the Windows Companion checks whether a poll from Relution Server is
necessary. Default is 180 minutes.

pollAfterMinutes.description:
Defines the minimum time in minutes the Windows Companion waits to execute the next poll request to Relution Server. Default is 120 minutes.

The Windows Companion compares this time to the time when it lastly requested the next action from Relution Server, independently whether that was triggered via poll or push.

manageWingetDependencies.description:
Specifies whether the Windows Companion manages WinGet and its dependencies, like VC Redistributable.

If true, the Windows Companion updates WinGet and all its dependencies when being started. If false, the Windows Companion does not update them. It will still install a missing VC Redistributable to make sure that WinGet commands always work.

installSyncML.description: SyncML to be executed when the configuration is applied on the device.

deleteSyncML.description: SyncML to be executed when the configuration is removed from the device.

wrapInAtomic.description:
Specifies if the SyncML is wrapped in an org.syncml.Atomic.

If false, it is wrapped in a org.syncml.Sequence.

accountType.description:
EMAIL2 - ACCOUNTTYPE
Specifies the type of account.
Only Email type is supported by us for now.

serviceName.description:
EMAIL2 - SERVICENAME
Character string that specifies the name of the email service to create or edit (32 characters maximum).
Replace command is not allowed.

serviceType.description:
EMAIL2 - SERVICETYPE
Character string that specifies the type of email service to create or edit. 
Replace command is not allowed.

emailAddress.description:
EMAIL2 - REPLYADDR
Character string that specifies the reply email address of the user.

senderName.description:
EMAIL2 - NAME
Character string that specifies the name of the sender displayed on a sent email.

incomingMailServerHostName.description:
EMAIL2 - INSERVER (togehter with incomingMailServerPort)
Character string that specifies the name of the incoming server name and port number.

incomingMailServerPort.description:
EMAIL2 - INSERVER (togehter with incomingMailServerHostName)
Character string that specifies the name of the incoming server name and port number.

incomingMailServerDomain.description:
EMAIL2 - DOMAIN
Character string that specifies the incoming server credentials domain.

incomingMailServerUseSSL.description:
EMAIL2 - TAGPROPS/8128000B
Character string that specifies if the incoming email server requires SSL.

syncPeriod.description:
EMAIL2 - DWNDAY
Character string that specifies how many days' worth of email should be downloaded from the server.

syncInterval.description:
EMAIL2 - LINGER
Character string that specifies the length of time between email send/receive updates in minutes.

attachmentMaxSize.description:
EMAIL2 - KEEPMAX
Specifies the maximum size for a message attachment in KB.
Attachments beyond this size will not be downloaded but it will remain on the server.
The message itself will be downloaded.
This value can be set only for IMAP4 accounts!.

messageMaxSize.description:
EMAIL2 - RETRIEVE
Specifies the maximum size in bytes for messages retrieved from the incoming email server.
Messages beyond this size are retrieved, but truncated.

serverDeleteActionType.description:
EMAIL2 - SERVERDELETEACTION
Character string that specifies how message is deleted on server.

cellularOnly.description:
EMAIL2 - CELLULARONLY
If this flag is set, the account only uses the cellular network and not Wi-Fi.

outgoingMailServerHostName.description:
EMAIL2 - OUTSERVER (together with outgoingMailServerPort)
Character string that specifies the name of the messaging service's outgoing email server (SMTP server).

outgoingMailServerPort.description:
EMAIL2 - OUTSERVER (together with outgoingMailServerHostName)
Number that specifies the port number of the messaging service's outgoing email server (SMTP server).

outgoingMailServerAuthRequired.description:
EMAIL2 - AUTHREQUIRED
Specifies whether the outgoing server requires authentication.

outgoingMailServerEnabled.description:
EMAIL2 - SMTPALTENABLED
Specifies if the user's alternate SMTP account is enabled. If disabled the same credentials as for incoming mail
server are used.

outgoingMailServerUsername.description:
EMAIL2 - SMTPALTAUTHNAME
Character string that specifies the display name associated with the user's alternative SMTP email account.
Only set if outgoingMailServerEnabled is true.

outgoingMailServerDomain.description:
EMAIL2 - SMTPALTDOMAIN
Character string that specifies the domain name for the user's alternative SMTP account.
Only set if outgoingMailServerEnabled is true.

outgoingMailServerUseSSL.description:
EMAIL2 - TAGPROPS/812C000B
Specifies if the outgoing email server requires SSL.

  • For PUBLIC_BROWSING the default is 5

Minutes Between backend and frontend in minutes
provide in seconds to the device.

smartCardRemovalBehavior.description: `null` means NOT_CONFIGURED, in this case property shouldn't be applied on the device.

auditIncomingNTLMTraffic.description: `null` means NOT_CONFIGURED, in this case property shouldn't be applied on the device.

useProxyScript.description: Represents the Proxy Auto Config (PAC) script.

allowSimplePassword.description:
DeviceLock/AllowSimpleDevicePassword. 
Specifies if PIN (not password) like "1111" or "1234" are allowed. For desktops it also controls the use of
picture password.

minLength.description:
DeviceLock/MinDevicePasswordLength. 
Specifies the minimum number or characters. Local Accounts will always enforce a minimum password length of 6.
Required in the PIN.
minLength = null means that no password is required.
Always use Replace command instead of Add for Windows10 desktop editions.

quality.description:
DeviceLock/AlphanumericDevicePasswordRequired. 
Determines the type of PIN (not password) required. This policy only applies if DevicedPasswordEnabled policy is
set to enabled.
Always use Replace command instead of Add for Windows10 desktop editions.

expiration.description:
DeviceLock/DevicePasswordExpiration. 
Specifies when the password expires (in days).

history.description:
DeviceLock/DevicePasswordHistory. 
Specifies how many passwords can be stored in the history that can’t be used.
Max value is not 50 as stated in documentation, but 24.

maxFailAttempts.description:
DeviceLock/MaxDevicePasswordFailedAttempts. 
The number of authentication failures before the device will be wiped (only mobile).
Windows desktop will be put on the BitLocker recovery mode, which makes the data inaccessible but recoverable.
BitLocker has to be enabled, otherwise this policy cannot apply.
Min and Max values are different for desktop (4 <= X <= 16) and mobile (0 <= X <= 999) devices.
0 means that the device is never wiped or set to recovery mode.

maxInactivityTime.description:
DeviceLock/MaxInactivityTimeDeviceLock. 
Specifies the amount of time (in minutes) after the device is idle that will cause the device to become PIN or
password locked.
0 means that no timeout is defined.

minPasswordAge.description:
DeviceLock/MinimumPasswordAge. 
Determines the period of time (in days) that a password must be used before the user can change it.
A value of zero means user does not need to change the password.
default maxPasswordAge (see GruppenRichtlinienEditor) is 42 and cannot be changed via policy csp, therefore
minPasswordAge cannot be higher than 41.
BUT if maxPasswordAge is changed to a value smaller than its default,
it could lead to a failing request when minPasswordAge ≥ maxPasswordAge!.

allowAddingForeignAccountsManually.description:
Accounts/AllowAddingNonMicrosoftAccountsManually. 
Specify whether user is allowed to add non MSA email accounts.

allowDeveloperUnlock.description:
ApplicationManagement/AllowDeveloperUnlock. 
Specify whether developer unlock is allowed at the device.

allowAllTrustedApps.description:
ApplicationManagement/AllowAllTrustedApps. 
Specify whether non Microsoft Store apps are allowed.

allowAppStoreAutoUpdate.description:
ApplicationManagement/AllowAppStoreAutoUpdate. 
Specify whether automatic update of apps from Microsoft Store are allowed.

allowGameDVR.description:
ApplicationManagement/AllowGameDVR
Specify whether DVR and broadcasting is allowed.

allowSharedUserAppData.description:
ApplicationManagement/AllowSharedUserAppData. 
Share application data among multiple users on the system and with other instances of that app.
Disabling does not delete any previously shared data on the SharedLocal folder.

blockNonAdminUserInstall.description:
ApplicationManagement/BlockNonAdminUserInstall. 
Manage non-administrator users' ability to install Windows app packages. Does not apply on Microsoft Store.

disableStoreOriginatedApps.description:
ApplicationManagement/DisableStoreOriginatedApps. 
Disable the launch of all apps from Microsoft Store that came pre-installed or were downloaded.

requirePrivateStoreOnly.description:
ApplicationManagement/RequirePrivateStoreOnly. 
Disable the retail catalog and only enable the Private store.

allowRoaming.description:
Connectivity/AllowCellularDataRoaming. 
Allow or disallow cellular data roaming.
0 (false) is not supported in WindowsVersion.V_1511. Therefore this property is only send for versions that are
newer.
In Future there could be a new annotation created to only "not support" one specific WindowsVersion f.e. V_1607.

allowVPNOverCellular.description:
Connectivity/AllowVPNOverCellular. 
This policy specifies what type of underline connections VPN is allowed
to use.

allowVPNRoamingOverCellular.description:
Connectivity/AllowVPNRoamingOverCellular. 
This policy when enforced, will prevent the device from connecting VPN
when the device roams over cellular networks.

allowBluetooth.description:
Connectivity/AllowBluetooth. 
Allow the user to enable Bluetooth or restrict access.

allowCortana.description:
Experience/AllowCortana. 
Specify whether Cortana is allowed at the device.

allowSyncMySettings.description:
Experience/AllowSyncMySettings. 
Allow enterprise to disallow roaming settings among devices (in/from WP
device). If not enforced, whether roaming is allowed or not could depend
on other factors. depends on other factors.

allowManualUnenrollment.description:
Experience/AllowManualMDMUnenrollment. 
Specify whether allow the user to delete the workplace account via
workplace control panel. The MDM server always could remotely delete the
account.

allowSettings.description:
Settings/PageVisibilityList. 
Allows the user to hide specific settings
Only the first field is annotated, since all properties are sent to the same endpoint
it only has to be checked once.

allowPowerAndSleep.description:
Settings/AllowPowerAndSleep. 
Allows the user to change power and sleep settings.

allowDateTime.description:
Settings/AllowDateTime. 
Allows the user to change date and time settings.

allowRegion.description:
Settings/AllowRegion. 
Allows the user to change region settings.

allowLanguage.description:
Settings/AllowLanguage. 
Allows the user to change language settings.

allowVPN.description:
Settings/AllowVPN
Allows the user to change VPN settings.

allowWorkplace.description:
Settings/AllowWorkplace. 
Allows user to change workplace settings.

allowYourAccount.description:
Settings/AllowYourAccount. 
Allows user to change account settings.

allowLocationType.description:
Force or allow location service, see System/AllowLocation
  • 0 – force off
  • 1 – allow (user can set it)
  • 2 - force on
.

allowUserToResetDevice.description:
System/AllowUserToResetPhone. 
Specify whether allow the user to factory reset the phone from setting
control panel and hardware key combination.

allowWiFi.description:
WiFi/AllowWiFi. 
Allow or disallow WiFi connection. (Configurable by Exchange as well –
definition will be consistent with EAS definition.)

allowInternetSharing.description:
WiFi/AllowInternetSharing. 
Allow or disallow internet sharing (Configurable by Exchange as well –
definition will be consistent with EAS definition.)

allowAutoConnectToHotspots.description:
WiFi/AllowAutoConnectToWiFiSenseHotspots. 
Allow or disallow the device to automatically connect to Wi-Fi hotspots
and friend social network.

allowManualWiFiConfiguration.description:
WiFi/AllowManualWiFiConfiguration. 
Allow or disallow connecting to Wi-Fi outside of MDM server-installed
networks.

scanMode.description:
Wifi/WLANScanMode. 
This policy defines the frequency mode for active Wi-Fi scanning trigger
when screen is off and on. High setting would result in faster/better
WiFi discoverbility.

eventTriggersEnabled.description: Enables the use of event based triggers.

eventTriggers.description: List of events to trigger the script execution.

scheduledTriggerEnabled.description: Enables the use of a time based trigger.

scheduledDays.description: List of days on which the script execution is triggered.

scheduledStartTime.description: The start time when the script execution is triggered on the specified days.

timeZone.description: The zone of the start time.

disallowStartIfOnBatteries.description:
When true, it disallows the start of a script execution if the device is running on batteries.

When false, the task is executed even if the device is running on batteries.

stopIfGoingOnBatteries.description:
When true, it stops the script execution task when the device is going onto batteries.

When false, the task is not stopped when the device is going onto batteries.

allowDemandStart.description:
When true, the script execution task can be started independent of a specified trigger.

When false, the task can only be started by defined triggeres.

wakeToRun.description:
When true, the Task Scheduler will wake the computer when it is time to run the script execution task.

When false, the Task Scheduler does not wake the computer.

hidden.description:
When true, the task is not visible in the Task Scheduler UI.

When false, the task is visible.

userId.description:
Windows User ID to specify a user for the LOGON event trigger to only execute scripts
when a specific user logs in.

startLayout.description: Represent Start layout XML file resource.

startLayoutJson.description: Represent Start layout XML file resource.

importEdgeAssets.description: Represent Import edge assets XML file resource.

productVersion.description:
Can provide updates for specified product versions, but must be used together with a valid releaseVersion. Otherwise, this property is ignored on the device.

Known valid values for productVersion are:

  • Windows 10
  • Windows 11
  • 11.

wipeAfterDays must be greater than blockAfterDays.

This allows to persist data for each reviewed object separately.

it won't ever change back to `false`.

Points to the workflow step, which was being approved or rejected when this app history was created. Lose
reference (not a FK), if the device is deleted the history will remain and the reference will point to the void.

Lose reference (not a FK), if the device is deleted the history will remain and the reference will point to the
void.

Payload date value of the app history, its purpose and meaning is context based.n

ARRANGE_RELEASE_DATE review step it represents the proposed release date of a
version.

vppLicensedInCurrentOrga.description: Should be used for display only, don't rely logic on this field.

key.description: The unique key identifying the state.

createTime.description: The timestamp indicating when the app state was created on the device.

lastUpdateTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the app state was last updated on the device.

message.description:
An optional string providing details about the app state. App developers are advised to treat this field as a
user-facing message.

severity.description:
The severity of the state: INFO indicates an informative message. For example if a managed configuration is set
successfully. ERROR indicates the enterprise needs to take action to correct a problem. For example, if a managed
configuration failed to set.

data.description:
An optional string providing computer-readable details to EMMs about the app state. For example, a value that an IT
admin could query against in your console, such as "notify me if the battery_warning data < 10".

notFound.description:

- app was just created in Google Play Store -> might take a few minutes
- OrganizationID is not linked with the app within Google Play Store.

requireUpdate.description:
If the version in the Google Play Store is different (usually less) than current version code, then an json update is
required.

latestUploadedVersion.description: Latest uploaded version in Google Play Store.

chunkOffset.description:
offset of next chunk the server is expecting.

  • with 0 indicating the very first chunk,
  • positive offsets mark the start of a range in the file uploaded, and
  • negative offsets indicating no more chunks once the full Resource was uploaded.

.

chunkSize.description: length of chunks the server expects, a server-side configuration option typically around 4MB.

provides OpenAPI metadata for multipart MIME uploads.

Multipart MIME content can be annotated using Operation as follows: 

@Operation(responses = {
@ApiResponse(content = @Content(schema = @Schema(type = "string", format = "binary")))
})
and 
@Operation(requestBody = @RequestBody(content = @Content(schema = @Schema(implementation = FileContent.class)), required = true))

.

CSVImportJob.description:
This entity keeps track of import jobs.
Jobs have a CSVImportJobStatus to track if it's running, done etc.
We also track count of successfully imported items as well as failed or skipped items.
Further info about the state of single entries can be tracked via CSVImportJobEntityReport.
Jobs have a lifetime of LIFETIME, a cleanup job takes care of this.

CSVPreviewEntityAutoEnrollmentUpdateCsvI.description:
This model was introduced to wrap CSV Import Preview results.
Since the data type differs on success/failure, the decision was made to support both.
A parsing failure (like unexpected number of elements) will be a "report".
A successful parse will end up in the expected entity.
Therefore, either one of them will be filled, but never both.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

version.description: Return Integer version of domain object.

foreignKey.description: gets the foreignKey of the 3rd party system.

name.description: Returns unique user name in the system.

ssoOnly.description:
Gets a value indicating whether the user is prohibited from using password based authentication.

SSO-only users do not have or require a password and cannot sign in directly. They can only sign in through an external Identity Provider (IdP), such as OpenID Connect or SAML. They can also use authentication tokens.

passwordChangeRequired.description: Is password change required on next login.

locked.description:
Gets a value indicating whether the user is locked.

A locked user can't sign in. This field is set by the security provider the user account is associated with.

activated.description:
Gets a value indicating whether the user is active.

A deactivated user can't sign in and doesn't receive push notifications and emails. This field can be set by an administrator through the web interface.

allowEmailAddressCollection.description: Gets a value indicating whether the user wants to receive email notifications.

confirmationTokenValidTo.description: Returns confirmation token with time of valid to.

minAndroidCompanionVersionCode.description:
The minimum version code of the Relution Companion app that should be installed on Android devices.

If a device does not have the app installed in the specified version, or newer, an available migration is shown in the migration center.

minAndroidCompanionVersion.description:
This is the informational minimum version of the Android Companion needed for migration.

This version is shown in the portal's migration wizard.

minAndroidAgentVersionCode.description:
The minimum version code of the Relution Agent app that should be installed on Android devices.

If a device does not have the app installed in the specified version, or newer, an available migration is shown in the migration center.

minAndroidAgentVersion.description:
This is the informational minimum version of the Android Agent needed for migration.

This version is shown in the portal's migration wizard.

cacheDuration.description:
The duration for which to cache query results.

The notification may intermittently show incorrect results when the number of devices to be migrated has changed and the previously cached query result is used for the notification.

migrationStatus.description:
Returns the migration status.
  • NONE — agent and/or companion are not installed in the desired version and no deploy app action is pending.
  • PENDING — companion is installed in the desired version or a deploy app action is pending
  • MIGRATED — companion and agent are installed in the desired versions.
.

Contains aggregate information for an InstalledApp.

Contains the platform, identifier and (localized) name of an installed app along with the number of devices that have the app installed. Optionally contains version information for the installed app.

Multiple (localized) entries for the same app can be merged to get the combined installation count.

DTO for

  • QUERY_INSTALLED_APPS_BY_PLATFORM
  • QUERY_INSTALLED_APP_VERSIONS_BY_PLATFORM
.

executionState.description: Describes whether the script execution was successful or not.

previewResult.description:
Contains the first 4000 characters of the result output of the script execution. The whole content is not saved here for better performance when querying the database.

A script can be executed with an error, but contain partial successful output.

previewError.description:
Contains the first 4000 characters of the error output of the script execution.

The whole content is not saved here for better performance when querying the database.

executionDate.description: Contains the date of the script execution on the device.

actionUuid.description:
Only used to display a link to the action in Portal. Is null, if the execution was triggered by a configuration.

Foreign key cannot be saved for MS SQL database due to possible cascading cycles.

policyUuid.description:
Only used to display a link to the policy in Portal. Is null, if the execution was triggered by an action.

Is set to null by foreign key when corresponding policy is deleted from database. No JPA mapping is added, to not unnecessarily fetch the policy when loading script execution results (lazy loading is not enough).

policyVersionUuid.description:
Only used to display a link to the policy version in Portal. Is null, if the execution was triggered by an action.

Is set to null by foreign key when corresponding policy version is deleted from database. No JPA mapping is added, to not unnecessarily fetch the policy version when loading script execution results (lazy loading is not enough).

configurationUuid.description:
Only used to display a link to the configuration in Portal. Is null, if the execution was triggered by an action.

Is set to null by foreign key when corresponding configuration is deleted from database. No JPA mapping is added, to not unnecessarily fetch the configuration when loading script execution results (lazy loading is not enough).

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

EnrollmentAgreement.description:
Agreement which can be attached to enrollments and
{@link io.relution.devices.autoenrollment.AutoEnrollment DEP auto enrollments}.

name.description:
See getName()
The name of the enrollment token, which is generated by the server during creation, in the form
enterprises/{enterpriseId}/enrollmentTokens/{enrollmentTokenId}.

policyName.description:
See getPolicyName()
The name of the policy initially applied to the enrolled device, in the form
enterprises/{enterpriseId}/policies/{policyId}. If not specified, the policy_name for the
device’s user is applied. If user_name is also not specified,
enterprises/{enterpriseId}/policies/default is applied by default. When updating this field,
you can specify only the policyId as long as the policyId doesn’t contain any slashes. The rest
of the policy name will be inferred.

expirationTimestamp.description:
See getExpirationTimestamp()
The expiration time of the token. This is a read-only field generated by the server.

qrCode.description:
See getQrCode()
A JSON string whose UTF-8 representation can be used to generate a QR code to enroll a device
with this enrollment token. To enroll a device using NFC, the NFC record must contain a
serialized java.util.Properties representation of the properties in the JSON.

value.description:
See getValue()
The token value that's passed to the device and authorizes the device to enroll. This is a
read-only field generated by the server.

userAccountIdentifier.description:
See getAccountIdentifier()
A unique identifier you create for this user, such as user342 or asset#44418. This field must
be set when the user is created and can't be updated. This field must not contain personally
identifiable information (PII). This identifier must be 1024 characters or less; otherwise, the
update policy request will fail.

oneTimeOnly.description:
See getOneTimeOnly()
Whether the enrollment token is for one time use only. If the flag is set to true, only one
device can use it for registration.

wifiSecurityType.description: Gets extra indicating the security type of the wifi network in EXTRA_PROVISIONING_WIFI_SSID and could be one of NONE, WPA, WEP or EAP.Should be used with property name android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_WIFI_SECURITY_TYPE in QR code JSON. For more details see EXTRA_PROVISIONING_WIFI_SECURITY_TYPE

wifiSsid.description: Gets optional wifi network ssid used through the enrollment process via QR code.Should be used with property name android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_WIFI_SSID in QR code JSON. For more details see EXTRA_PROVISIONING_WIFI_SSID

wifiHidden.description: Idicating whether the wifi network in PROVISIONING_WIFI_SSID is hidden or not.

localeCode.description: Gets String extra holding the Locale that the device will be set to. Format: xx_yy, where xx is the language code, and yy the country code. Should be used with property name android.app.extra.PROVISIONING_LOCALE in QR code JSON. For more details see EXTRA_PROVISIONING_LOCALE

keepSystemAppsEnabled.description:
A Boolean extra that can be used by the mobile device management application to skip the disabling of system apps
during provisioning when set to true.

user.description:
Returns the User UUID or null if no explicit user is
assigned to the enrollment.

authenticationRequired.description:
Determines whether authentication is required during the enrollment process. Only available for
{@link Platform#ANDROID_ENTERPRISE Android Enterprise} enrollments. Not maintained and synced with
isAuthenticationRequired().

oneTimeOnly.description: MDM-20087: If the enrollment can be used just once, or for enrolling multiple devices.

claimAfterEnrollment.description:
Flag that indicates whether the serial number of a device should be claimed after successful enrollment. This is
required in case the serial number is unknown before the enrollment.

BulkEnrollment.description:
BulkEnrollment object is used for coupling of processed. 
Enrollment with processing information. This way it is possible to
indicate the result of bulk enrollment process.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

platform.description: Optional platform for static groups to accept only a specific type of devices.

deviceFilter.description:
optional filter of devices turning this into a dynamic group.

Notice, this property is a lazy-loaded CLOB that is supplied to the UI upon explicit request as a GetQuery field
only. This is so that paginated lists do not take a performance hit.

devices.description:
devices in the group.

Notice, the server updates the set of devices by evaluating the deviceFilter upon saving a dynamic group only.

DeviceGroup.description:
named group of Device members typically serving UI and/or auto-configuration purposes.

Static device groups have the list of member devices populated explicitly. The Device members must belong to
the same organization as the group itself.

Dynamic device groups are defined by means of a deviceFilter expression tree. All devices of the same organization as
the group that are matched by the filter are group members.

deviceUuids.description: Device uuids to set for the DeviceGroup.

policyUuids.description: Policy uuids to set for the DeviceGroup.

policyVersionState.description: The state of the policy version that should be used by the device group.

uuid.description: Return unique identifier for domain object.

autoAssignTo.description:
Gets the unique identifiers of the roles the policy should be automatically assigned to during enrollment.

The unique identifiers specified here should only be used when setAutoAssignment(AutoAssignment) is set to SPECIFIED_ROLES. Otherwise, they should be ignored.

The policy should be automatically assigned when a new device is enrolled and the unique identifier of the user assigned to the device is contained in this collection, or the user is a member of a group whose unique identifier is contained in the collection.

scheduledEnabled.description:
Does this policy follow a weekly, local-time schedule, where it is active/inactive depending on the day of the
week and time of day.

offTimeEnabled.description: Should this policy be inactive during off-times, for example during public holidays.

scheduleState.description:
Details to the state of the schedule and / or current organization's OffTimes.

These details are context dependant (orga and time), and cannot be persisted.

These details should be null, until intentionally filled, to avoid unnecessary overhead when fetching policies. For example, if the portal requests policies with the scheduleState.

state.description:
The state of this policy version. Only one policy version per policy can be in DRAFT or. 
PUBLISHED.

Request headers as defined in RFC-2616: one header per line, header and value separated by colon, value with optional

WebHookRequest.description:
describes an arbitrary HTTP request.

Placeholder values of the device are supported in url, headers and body properties.
An absolute url is required, which may optionally include username and password for basic authentication.
The following headers are supplied with suitable default values, but may be overwritten in headers
explicitly: User-Agent, Date, Authentication (extracted from url).

enableStayOnAcCharging.description:
The following three StayOn properties are booleans instead a list of enums, to avoid having to add a
table for this configuration. Unlike the. 
AndroidEnterpriseKioskModeConfiguration, these
values are not stored as a json in the database. To use the same implementation, as a list of enums, this
configuration would require either a converter that can store the list as a comma separated list of strings in the
database. Or the LessonEntity would need to be refactored to have an @Entity annotation, which would require
additional refactoring of the Lesson and LessonTemplate table.

whitelisted for the students.

webLinkUuids.description:
Uuids of the webclip apps that should be whitelisted
for the students.

ownerUuid.description: The uuid of the teacher of the lesson.

allowCamera.description:
whether `allowCamera` restriction should be set and CAMERA should be part of the system
apps list.

allowSafari.description: When false, the Safari web browser application is disabled and its icon removed from the Home screen. This also prevents users from opening web clips. This key is deprecated on unsupervised devices.

allowAppStore.description:
if `true` sets `allowAppInstallation` and `allowUIAppInstallation` in the restriction configuration to `true`, otherwise these fields will be set to `false`

In addition, if `true`, APP_STORE will be added to the ios system apps list and otherwise be removed.

allowItunes.description:
When false, the iTunes Music Store is disabled and its icon is removed from the Home screen. Users cannot preview, purchase, or download content.

In addition, if `true`, ITUNES_STORE will be added to the ios system apps list and otherwise be removed.

allowMessages.description: Whether MESSAGES should be part of ios system apps.

allowKeyboardHelp.description:
Whether `allowPredictiveKeyboard`, `allowAutoCorrection` and `allowSpellCheck`
should be enabled in the restrictions configuration.

allowAirDrop.description: If set to false, AirDrop is disabled.

privateLesson.description: whether the lesson is private or not.

enableRestrictedPlayStore.description:
if enabled, only apps in the configuration are available and any app not in the configuration will automatically uninstalled from the device.

Note: Android Enterprise devices only.

enforceBluetooth.description: If active, bluetooth will be enabled on supported devices on lesson start.

androidSingleAppMode.description: Details for the Single app mode for android.

iosSingleAppMode.description: Details for the Single app mode for iOS.

weblinks.description: Weblinks stored only for this lesson.

weblink URLs only.

Relevant when the Lesson actually has weblinks configured only.

enableDeconConfiguration.description: Whether the deconConfiguration should be active for the lesson.

disableRestrictions.description: If enabled no lesson specific restrictions are applied.

Group classifies datatype that the filter can be applied to (eg. "device", "user", "log").

FilterHoldersWrapper.description: Wrapper array of FilterHolder's for export to xml.

OrganizationDomain.description:
An organization to (mail) domain name mapping for
login purposes.

A domain can be mapped to one organization only. Upon login, the user email domain is checked and when it matches an. 
OrganizationDomain record the user is redirected to the login page of the organization associated. Exact
matches count only, a domain name configured does not match and incl. subdomains because ownership of a domain does
not imply subdomain ownerships.

AccessToken.description:
Access tokens which can be used to acquire a user's authorization context when provided as value in the HTTP header `X-User-Access-Token` (see USER_ACCESS_TOKEN_HTTP_HEADER)

Version 2 (Current Version):n Tokens consist of an identifier part (14 characters) and a secret part (46 characters) which are generated randonmly using a java.security.SecureRandom. The identifier part is used to find the corresponding stored token. The secret part is compaired against the stored version salted and hashed via hashpw.

The following older token versions do exist:

Version 0:n Legacy tokens for which UUIDs were used. Their unsalted SHA-1 hash had been stored. Tokens of this version will no longer exists after db migration introducing the newer versions.

Version 1:n The migrated version of version 0 tokens. The first 14 characters of the stored SHA-1 hash are used as identifier while the full stored hash is used as secret which is then hashed with hashpw.

.

LdapOrganizationMapping.description:
Maps synchronized LDAP objects to a sub-organization.

Mapping filters defined by this instance allow users, groups and courses imported by one organization to be persisted in another organization instead. This functionality is generally only available to the global organization and meta-organizations and can only be used to redirect imported objects to their respective sub-organizations.

The mapping belongs to the organization its getLdapConfiguration() is associated with; i.e. the organization that executes the import. The {@link io.relution.security.ldap.configuration.LdapOrganizationMapping#getMappedOrganization mapped organization} associated with this mapping determines the organization objects should be imported into.

Note that mapping filters do not import LDAP objects. They can only be used to reassign objects that match an import filter to another organization before they are persisted.

This means that, for example, a user must match the user import filter to be imported before the organization mapping filter then determines which organization the imported user should belong to. If an object only matches a mapping filter, but does not match an import filter, it is not imported.

If an object matches an import filter, but does not match a mapping filter it may also be rejected, if the {@link LdapOrganizationMappingSettings#isSyncUnmappedObjectsToDefaultOrganization sync unmapped objects} setting is set to `false`. Otherwise, if this setting is `true`, the object is imported into the organization executing the LDAP import instead.

Returns the unique identifier of the organization the assignment belongs to.

The organization an assignment belongs to can be different from the associated VPP token's organization. We try to fill the organizationUuid, by setting it to the organizationUuid of the corresponding device or user. When we can not find a corresponding device or user the organizationUuid is set to the organizationUuid is set to the organizationUuid of the VPP token.

This is useful for public VPP tokens. Regular organizations should only see the assignments belonging to users and devices of their organization. The store organization should see the assignments belonging to users and devices of all organizations.

The organization a vpp user belongs to can be different from the associated VPP token's organization. We try to fill the organizationUuid, by setting it to the corresponding users organizationUuid. When we can not find a corresponding user the organizationUuid is set to the organizationUuid of the VPP token.

This is useful for public VPP tokens. Regular organizations should only see the vpp users belonging to their organization. The store organization should see the vpp users belonging to all organizations.

properties.name: relution.android.enterprise.max-requests-per-second
properties.description:
The maximum number of requests per second to send.

This is a per-server limit, that attempts to avoid exceeding Google's limit of 1000 queries in 100 seconds for the Android Management API.

While the API allows for bursts of up-to 1000 queries within a 100-second window, this limit can be roughly translated to 10 queries per second (10 QPS).

Since multiple servers share this limit and servers run independently of one another, we use a more conservative limit of 2 QPS by default, or 20% of the allowable maximum.

Combined with a warmup period of 60 seconds, this allows for bursts of up to 120 queries, if the rate limiter has been idle for at least one minute prior. Once the maximum number of stored permits have been used, the rate limiter will limit subsequent requests to a stable throughput of 2 QPS. @see Permissible usage

properties.name: relution.android.enterprise.min-requests-per-second
properties.description:
The minimum number of API requests per second.

If the server is running in a cluster, the maximum number of requests is divided between cluster nodes. The minimum defines the lowest value for requests per second. @see #maxRequestsPerSecond

properties.name: relution.android.enterprise.post-enrollment-auto-policy-refresh-after
properties.description:
The amount of time to wait, after a device first reports itself as active, before a policy refresh is triggered automatically.

In some cases newly enrolled devices do not install apps for a long time, even though Google reports installation as "IN_PROGRESS". Applying the same policy on the device again appears to fix it.

properties.name: relution.android.enterprise.rate-limit-warmup-period
properties.description: The warmup period during which the number of requests per second is smoothly increased until the configured maximum is reached.

properties.name: relution.education.lesson-information.batch-size
properties.description:
The maximum number of students (per lesson) to process per batch.

When updating dynamic lesson information, this value determines the maximum number of students for which data such as concurrent active lessons and device related information such as open actions is fetched from the database at once.

Increasing this number reduces the number of database round trips, but potentially increases the amount of RAM required. Decreasing this number increases the number of database round trips, but potentially decreases the amount of RAM required.

A high number of database round trips significantly increases processing time due to network overhead. @see io.relution.education.lesson.DynamicLessonInformationService#updateDynamicLessonInformation(Lesson)

Default runs every day at 23:10. Beware that Spring Cron expressions contain seconds, but not every implementation of cron does. 

 ┌───────────── second (0-59) │ ┌───────────── minute (0 - 59) │ │ ┌───────────── hour (0 - 23) │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the month (1 - 31) │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── month (1 - 12) (or JAN-DEC) │ │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the week (0 - 7) │ │ │ │ │ │ (or MON-SUN -- 0 or 7 is Sunday) │ │ │ │ │ │ 0 10 23 * * *

properties.name: relution.ldap.health-status-cache-duration
properties.description: The minimum amount of time the result of checking a directory server's connection status is cached. @see SecurityProviderLdapHealthService @see io.relution.security.ldap.health.LdapHealthContributor

properties.name: relution.ldap.max-schema-cache-size
properties.description:
The maximum number of directory server schemas to hold in memory.

It is expected that an {@link SecurityProviderLdap LDAP provider} typically only uses a single schema across all import types (users, groups, course, teachers and students). This means the cache should be able to hold schemas for this number of providers.

If two providers use the same LDAP server, the cache will only hold a single copy of the schema for both of them, further increasing the number of providers able to be serviced by the same cache entry. A schema is only cached if a provider needs access to it at runtime (i.e. currently only when a provider distributes imported objects to other organizations; only when organization(…)Filters are defined) @see LdapSchemaCache

properties.name: relution.ldap.max-schema-reference-cache-size
properties.description:
The maximum number of references to a directory server schema to hold in memory.

Each {@link SecurityProviderLdap LDAP provider} has up to five schema references. However, these references typically point to the same schema. As such this number should generally be the schema cache size, times five. @see LdapSchemaCache

properties.name: relution.ldap.schema-cache-duration
properties.description:
The minimum amount of time a schema reference should remain in the cache after it was last accessed.

Schema references that have not been accessed for this amount of time become eligible for eviction from the cache. A schema will remain in the cache as long as at least one reference exists. Only when all references are gone a schema may be removed from the cache.

properties.name: relution.license.client-app-stats-expiration
properties.description: Time before client app statistics entries expire and are recomputed.

properties.name: relution.log.device-debug-logs.connect-timeout
properties.description: The connection timeout. For intra-cluster communication scenario, the nodes are generally within the same network and hence the latency should be quite low.

properties.name: relution.log.device-debug-logs.max-in-memory-size
properties.description: The maximum number of bytes that can be buffered whenever the input stream needs to be aggregated. @see org.springframework.http.codec.ClientCodecConfigurer.ClientDefaultCodecs#maxInMemorySize(int)

properties.name: relution.log.device-debug-logs.max-log-events-per-device
properties.description: The maximum number of log events that will be kept in memory for a single device

properties.name: relution.log.device-debug-logs.read-timeout
properties.description: The read timeout for receiving the logs from another cluster node as streamed chunks.

properties.name: relution.mdm.android.migration.cache-duration
properties.description:
The duration for which to cache query results.

The notification may intermittently show incorrect results when the number of devices to be migrated has changed and the previously cached query result is used for the notification.

properties.name: relution.mdm.android.migration.min-android-agent-version
properties.description:
This is the informational minimum version of the Android Agent needed for migration.

This version is shown in the portal's migration wizard.

properties.name: relution.mdm.android.migration.min-android-agent-version-code
properties.description:
The minimum version code of the Relution Agent app that should be installed on Android devices.

If a device does not have the app installed in the specified version, or newer, an available migration is shown in the migration center.

properties.name: relution.mdm.android.migration.min-android-companion-version
properties.description:
This is the informational minimum version of the Android Companion needed for migration.

This version is shown in the portal's migration wizard.

properties.name: relution.mdm.android.migration.min-android-companion-version-code
properties.description:
The minimum version code of the Relution Companion app that should be installed on Android devices.

If a device does not have the app installed in the specified version, or newer, an available migration is shown in the migration center.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.app-installation.management-flags.remove-apps-on-mdm-profile-removal
properties.description: Indicates how to handle the removal of apps when removing the MDM profile from the device.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.app-installation.status-refresh.interval
properties.description: The interval between two app status refreshes.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.app-installation.status-refresh.max-retries
properties.description: The maximum amount retries to refresh apps status information if an app is still in status installing.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.automatic-first-installation-retry.enabled
properties.description: whether the installation retry is enabled.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.automatic-first-installation-retry.last-try-offset
properties.description: The time that must have past between the last installation try before an app is eligible to be re-tried.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.challenge-password-expiration
properties.description: Specifies how long the challenge tokens are valid. Note: shouldn't be to short, since the iOS user enrollment might take a while, as the user first needs to enter its Managed AppleID and password to proceed. This will also be the validity period of the SCEP challenge password from the Return-to-Service payload.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.dep.lost-mode.message

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.identity-cert.renew-if-expiring-within
properties.description: If a device's identity certificate will expire within the given period, it will be eligible to be auto-updated as part of the next batch of devices.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.identity-cert.renewal-batch-size
properties.description: Max. number of devices that receive an MDM Profile update per {@link #renewalInterval}.

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.identity-cert.renewal-initial-delay
properties.description: The initial delay before the first batch of devices will have their identity cert renewed (if necessary).

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.identity-cert.renewal-interval
properties.description: The interval in which to renew the identity cert of the next batch of devices. The maximum amount of devices to check per interval is determined by {@link #renewalBatchSize}

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.ios.migration.cache-duration

properties.name: relution.mdm.apple.legacy-signature-fingerprints

properties.name: relution.mdm.device-groups.device-filter-period
properties.description: device filter is evaluated against database once every period detecting incorrectly associated device members.

properties.name: relution.mdm.device-groups.retry-interval
properties.description: time after which applying changes to a device in case of failure is retried.

properties.name: relution.mdm.policies.scheduled-policy-cycle-interval
properties.description: interval at which scheduled policies are evaluated.

properties.name: relution.mdm.policies.scheduled-policy-cycle-timeout

properties.name: relution.mdm.windows.max-expected-companion-response-time
properties.description:
Max expected time until the Windows Companion is detected as missing.

Default is 3 days.

properties.name: relution.mdm.windows.max-in-memory-size
properties.description: The maximum number of bytes that can be buffered whenever the input stream needs to be aggregated. @see org.springframework.http.codec.ClientCodecConfigurer.ClientDefaultCodecs#maxInMemorySize(int)

properties.name: relution.mdm.windows.not-now-action-wait-time
properties.description: Time in Minutes after which the NOT_NOW action will be automatically resolved

properties.name: relution.push.apns.client.max-ttl
properties.description:
The maximum duration for which to keep an APNS client around.

Whenever the maximum duration expires, a new instance of the client is created the next time a push message is sent. The client is also recreated when an exception occurs.

This setting has no actual effect unless the duration is shorter than {@link #getMaxEventLoopTtl()}.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.client.recreate-on-error
properties.description: Indicates whether the APNS client instance should be recreated when an exception occurs.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.client.suppress-repeat-keystore-error-log-duration
properties.description:
Indicates how long repeat error messages about an invalid/expired keystore should be snoozed before they can be logged again.

If multiple push notifications are sent in quick succession and the keystore is expired, this can result in multiple redundant error log messages. This setting suppresses the repeat log message for the specified duration after it is logged once.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.event-loop-group-size
properties.description: The size of the APNS Netty event loop group

properties.name: relution.push.apns.max-event-loop-ttl
properties.description:
The maximum duration for which to keep an event loop around.

Whenever the maximum duration expires, a new instance of the event loop is created the next time a push message is sent. This will also recreate all {@link ApnsCommunicationService} instances using the event loop.

If the maximum TTL for the APNS client is longer than this value, then the APNS client is effectively never recreated separately, because the service is recreated sooner. @see ApnsClientProperties#getMaxTtl()

properties.name: relution.push.apns.periodic-push.enabled
properties.description: Indicates whether periodic push is enabled.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.periodic-push.initial-delay
properties.description: The initial delay before the first periodic push is performed.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.periodic-push.interval
properties.description: The time to wait between periodic pushes, after {@code #initialDelay} has elapsed.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.periodic-push.max-devices-per-interval
properties.description: The maximum number of devices to notify per periodic push interval.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.periodic-push.min-last-push-time
properties.description: The minimum amount of time that must have passed since the last MDM push message to a device before another push message is sent.

properties.name: relution.push.apns.periodic-push.per-device-limit
properties.description:
Maximum age of the device's last action to be included.

Devices are only included in periodic push if their last action was created less than {@code perDeviceLimit} ago.

properties.name: relution.push.queue.consecutive-error-threshold
properties.description: During the {@link #loggingDelay}, if the {@link MdmPushQueue} grows for this many times consecutively an {@code error} will be logged instead of {@code debug}

properties.name: relution.push.queue.consecutive-warning-threshold
properties.description: During the {@link #loggingDelay}, if the {@link MdmPushQueue} grows for this many times consecutively a {@code warn} will be logged instead of {@code debug}.

properties.name: relution.push.queue.health-window-size
properties.description: Specifies the rolling window size of the health indicator endpoint tracked. The health values will be updated before the status is logged during the {@link #loggingDelay}.

properties.name: relution.push.queue.logging-delay
properties.description: Delay for which the {@link MdmPushQueueHealthService} determines and logs the current state of the {@link MdmPushQueue}.

properties.name: relution.push.queue.logging-threshold
properties.description: Specifies at which size of the {@link MdmPushQueue} the server starts logging its size

properties.name: relution.push.queue.misfire-like-threshold
properties.description:
Used to classify Quartz triggers as "misfire-like" for metrics.

Any trigger whose {@code NEXT_FIRE_TIME} lies at least this duration in the past (relative to the current time) is counted as a misfire.

properties.name: relution.push.sse.connected-client-limit
properties.description: maximum number of connected clients before the server starts dropping connections to protect itself against running out of TCP client ports.

properties.name: relution.push.sse.keepalive-interval
properties.description:
server sends an SSE comment message at this rate to keep the connection alive.

This relates to NGINX {@literal proxy_read_timeout} which defaults to 60 seconds. For safety, the default value provided here is a little less than that minute.

The keepalive may be disabled entirely by setting this property to {@code null}.

properties.name: relution.push.sse.max-random-jitter
properties.description: when notifying devices via SSE a random delay is applied to avoid causing a DDoS.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.connect-timeout
properties.description: The connection timeout.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.periodic-push.enabled
properties.description: Indicates whether periodic push is enabled.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.periodic-push.initial-delay
properties.description: The initial delay before the first periodic push is performed.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.periodic-push.interval
properties.description: The time to wait between periodic pushes, after {@code #initialDelay} has elapsed.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.periodic-push.max-devices-per-interval
properties.description: The maximum number of devices to notify per periodic push interval.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.periodic-push.min-last-push-time
properties.description: The minimum amount of time that must have passed since the last MDM push message to a device before another push message is sent.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.periodic-push.per-device-limit
properties.description:
Maximum age of the device's last action to be included.

Devices are only included in periodic push if their last action was created less than {@code perDeviceLimit} ago.

properties.name: relution.push.wns.read-timeout
properties.description: The read timeout.

properties.name: relution.remote-support.oem-connection-history-interval
properties.description: Teamviewer OEM connection history processing interval.

properties.name: relution.resources.itms.cache-duration
properties.description: The initial cache duration for an object added into the {@link ItmsSessionCache}. @see #minCacheDurationAfterRead

properties.name: relution.resources.itms.min-cache-duration-after-read
properties.description: Cache duration is set to this value after a successful read (i.e. cache hit), if remaining duration is below this value.

properties.name: relution.scep.ca-cert-priv-key-password
properties.description: CA Certificate Private Key Password

properties.name: relution.scep.ca-cert-renew-if-expiring-within
properties.description: If the SCEP CA certificate is expiring within the given period, it will be renewed as part of the renewal procedure.

properties.name: relution.scep.ca-cert-validation-cron
properties.description:
Cron expression on when to run the job to validate the SCEP CA certificate.

Default runs nightly at 3:00 AM. Beware that Spring Cron expressions contain seconds, but not every implementation of cron does. 

 ┌───────────── second (0-59) │ ┌───────────── minute (0 - 59) │ │ ┌───────────── hour (0 - 23) │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the month (1 - 31) │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── month (1 - 12) (or JAN-DEC) │ │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the week (0 - 7) │ │ │ │ │ │ (or MON-SUN -- 0 or 7 is Sunday) │ │ │ │ │ │ 0 0 5 1,15 * *

properties.name: relution.scep.challenge-password-timeout
properties.description: Challenge Password Timeout [s]

properties.name: relution.scep.key-store
properties.description: Key Store

properties.name: relution.scep.key-store-alias
properties.description: CA Certificate / Key Alias

properties.name: relution.scep.not-after-interval
properties.description:
Not After Interval of Certificate Validity [s]

default: 1 year

properties.name: relution.scep.not-before-interval
properties.description:
Not Before Interval of Certificate Validity [s]

default: one day

properties.name: relution.security.impersonation.enabled
properties.description:
whether impersonation via the portal UI is enabled.

This guards access to {@link AuthenticationService#impersonate(ImpersonateObject)} by limiting the authority

properties.name: relution.self-service.last-device-info-refresh-delay
properties.description: Defines after which period of time since the last refresh date the server allows triggering new actions of type {@link ActionType#REFRESH_DEVICE_INFO} for a device via self-service portal. @see io.relution.selfservice.SelfServicePortalService#refreshDeviceInfoOnCurrentUsersDevices(BulkRequest)

properties.name: relution.server.jwt-secure-flag-enabled
properties.description: If {@code false} the JWT cookie may only be transmitted using a secure connection. In general a browser will not send the cookie if the connection is HTTP. If the request was sent via localhost, false will be always set

properties.name: relution.server.min-delay-between-dumps
properties.description: Determines how long to wait before another thread/heap dump can be created on repeat errors.

properties.name: relution.shared-device.scheduled-logout-interval
properties.description: The interval time to check the devices for scheduled logout

properties.name: relution.shared-device.scheduled-logout-limit
properties.description: The limit of number of devices to be retrieved

properties.name: relution.smg.connect-timeout
properties.description: The connection timeout.

properties.name: relution.smg.connection-keep-alive
properties.description: connection keep alive of connections in the pool.

properties.name: relution.smg.connection-request-timeout
properties.description:
The timeout used when requesting a connection from the connection manager.

Defaults to {@code null} to use the system default.

properties.name: relution.smg.context-timeout
properties.description:
The timeout for the {@link jakarta.servlet.AsyncContext}.

Should be greater than or equal to {@link #socketTimeout}. If

properties.name: relution.smg.http-version-policy
properties.description: The HTTP version policy, {@code null} for default.

properties.name: relution.smg.initial-window-size
properties.description:
The maximum number of bytes accepted by {@code AbstractHttp1StreamDuplexer}, before the {@code ExchangeResponseConsumer}s {@link AsyncDataConsumer#updateCapacity(CapacityChannel) update capacity} method is invoked to query whether more data can be accepted. To avoid buffer overflows, this value should be less than or equal to {@code responseQueueSize * responseChannelCapacityIncrement}.

If this value is set too high, there's effectively no flow rate control (i.e. no backpressure).

properties.name: relution.smg.max-per-route-connections
properties.description: The maximum number of connections that can be used by a single route.

properties.name: relution.smg.max-total-connections
properties.description: The maximum number of total connections the proxy can hold open to backends.

properties.name: relution.smg.pool-concurrency-policy
properties.description:
Specifies whether limit guarantees are strictly observed.

A {@link PoolConcurrencyPolicy#LAX} policy allows for better concurrency, but maximums may not be strictly observed.

properties.name: relution.smg.pool-reuse-policy
properties.description: Specifies the order in which connections in the pool are re-used.

properties.name: relution.smg.request-queue-buffer-length
properties.description: The maximum number of bytes to read into memory at once, when a {@link HttpServletRequest}s input stream has data available. @see #requestQueueSize @see jakarta.servlet.ServletInputStream#setReadListener(ReadListener)

properties.name: relution.smg.request-queue-size
properties.description:
The maximum number of request buffers to keep in-memory, per request.

Any time a {@link HttpServletRequest} has data available, it is buffered in-memory until the associated {@link AsyncEntityProducer} requests it. If more data becomes available in the meantime, at most {@code requestQueueSize * requestQueueBufferLength} bytes are held in memory. @see #requestQueueBufferLength @see AbstractBinAsyncEntityProducer#produceData(StreamChannel)

properties.name: relution.smg.response-channel-capacity-increment
properties.description:
The number of bytes the consumer signals as ready to accept any time the {@link ServletOutputStream} is {@link ServletOutputStream#isReady() ready}.

This value is provided to the {@link AsyncDataConsumer}s {@link CapacityChannel} when {@link AsyncDataConsumer#updateCapacity(CapacityChannel)} is invoked and the stream subsequently becomes ready. @see #requestQueueSize @see jakarta.servlet.ServletInputStream#setReadListener(ReadListener)

properties.name: relution.smg.response-queue-size
properties.description:
The maximum number of byte buffers to cache in-memory, per response, before capacity updates are stopped.

Any time an {@link AsyncDataConsumer} has data available it is written to the {@link ServletOutputStream}, while the stream is {@link ServletOutputStream#isReady() ready} to accept writes without blocking. Otherwise, the data is buffered in-memory until the stream is ready again. To limit memory usage, the consumer attempts to keep byte buffers cached in-memory below this value, but that is not guaranteed.

If more data has already been received, it is cached in-memory regardless of this limit. The limit is solely used to control backpressure. Once the cached byte buffers exceeds this value, the consumer stops issuing capacity updates until the cache is empty. @see #responseChannelCapacityIncrement @see AsyncDataConsumer#consume(ByteBuffer)

properties.name: relution.smg.socket-timeout
properties.description: The socket timeout.

properties.name: relution.smg.suppressed-request-headers
properties.description: Request headers to be removed explicitly.

properties.name: relution.smg.time-to-live
properties.description: Defines the total span of time connections can be kept alive or execute requests.

properties.name: relution.smg.validate-after-inactivity
properties.description:
Defines period of inactivity after which persistent connections must be re-validated prior to being leased to the consumer.

Negative values passed to this method disable connection validation.

properties.name: relution.smg.yield-request-thread-after-cycles
properties.description:
The maximum number of read/write cycles to perform before yielding Apache's dispatcher thread.

If Undertow's input stream continuously provides data and Apache's channel is always ready to consume data, the dispatcher thread that invoked {@link AsyncEntityProducer#produce(DataStreamChannel)} remains in use until transfer completes. While optimal for the transfer's throughput it can negatively impact total system throughput by monopolizing the thread for a long time.

By imposing a limit, the produce method may return early, allowing other transfers to continue in the meantime before Apache invokes produce again.

properties.name: relution.storage.max-file-size
properties.description: Limits the maximum allowed resource upload size, defaulting to 1GB.

properties.name: relution.storage.max-file-sizes
properties.description: Limits the maximum allowed resource upload size for the given type.

properties.name: relution.storage.resource-storage-type
properties.description:
Resource Storage Type

Type of storage to use for resources. After changing the storage type for the first time and restarting the instance, the resources will be moved to this storage. Changing the storage type afterwards make the resources unavailable!

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.client-side-encryption.kms-access-key
properties.description: Optional: alternate credentials for the KMS client. If not provided, the S3 credentials will be used.

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.client-side-encryption.kms-key-id
properties.description: Specifies a KMS key to use for key wrapping/ unwrapping. Any valid KMS key identifier (including the full ARN or an alias ARN) is permitted.

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.client-side-encryption.kms-region
properties.description: Optional: alternate region for the KMS client. If not provided, the S3 region will be used.

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.client-side-encryption.kms-secret-key
properties.description: Optional: alternate credentials for the KMS client. If not provided, the S3 credentials will be used.

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.connectivity-check.api-call-attempt-timeout
properties.description:
Maximum time for a single network attempt. Short enough to fail quickly on network issues, long enough to allow one retry attempt if needed.

Note that no retry is attempted unless configured on the S3 client instance.

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.connectivity-check.api-call-timeout
properties.description: Maximum time the entire {@literal headBucket} request can take. Keep short so health failures are detected fast.

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.connectivity-check.enabled
properties.description:
Indicates whether availability of S3 storage should be checked periodically.

S3 storage availability is checked periodically if either this configuration property or {@literal management.health.s3.enabled} is set to {@code true}.

If both properties are {@code false}, no periodic checks are performed. If the health indicator is disabled, results of periodic checks have no influence on the server's overall health status. @see S3HealthIndicator @see S3HealthService

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.resource-migration-retry-wait-interval
properties.description: The time to wait before retrying to migrate resources.

properties.name: relution.storage.s3.resource-migration-schedule
properties.description: When to migrate resources, defaults to 3am.

properties.name: relution.storage.sql.block-size
properties.description:
when streaming resources the file content is retrieved in blocks of this size from the database.

It is important keeping this below 16MB to avoid the MariaDB driver copying data internally. This copying drives the JVM quickly out of memory. Notice, some threshold is necessary for protocol overhead, see {@link org.mariadb.jdbc.client.socket.impl.PacketReader#readPacket(boolean)}.

As of now 4MB blocks allow uploads to succeed when Relution is running in the IDE using 300MB heap configuration.

properties.name: relution.time.ntp-server.host-names
properties.description:
The list of NTP server host names (and optional ports) to query, may be empty.

The server will honor the order specified here initially, but may change the ordering based on error count and network round-trip. Subsequent queries prefer the server with the least amount of errors and fastest average response time.

properties.name: relution.time.ntp-server.query-schedule
properties.description:
Cron expression that determines when to check the server's time offset compared to the configured NTP server(s).

Runs every 15 minutes by default. Beware that Spring Cron expressions contain seconds, but not every implementation of cron does. 

 ┌───────────── second (0-59) │ ┌───────────── minute (0 - 59) │ │ ┌───────────── hour (0 - 23) │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the month (1 - 31) │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── month (1 - 12) (or JAN-DEC) │ │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the week (0 - 7) │ │ │ │ │ │ (or MON-SUN -- 0 or 7 is Sunday) │ │ │ │ │ │ 0 */15 * * * *

properties.name: relution.time.ntp-server.round-off-factor
properties.description:
Used to round the average delay to the closest multiple of the specified value.

By default, the system will round the average delay to the closest multiple of 25 (i.e. 0, 25, 50, 75, 100, …). This means time-servers with a roughly similar delay are considered equal, to ensure the system switches between them based on their query count.

properties.name: relution.time.ntp-server.socket-timeout
properties.description: The socket timeout to use when querying an NTP-server.

properties.name: relution.time.ntp-server.window-size
properties.description: The weight to give to previous requests when calculating the moving average of a server's delay.

properties.name: relution.time.offset-detection.threshold
properties.description:
The maximum drift between local time and time-server time that is considered acceptable.

If the observed drift exceeds this value, a warning is logged and shown as a server notification.

properties.name: relution.tracing.headers
properties.description: Headers to be logged

properties.name: relution.vpp.access-token-recreation-cron
properties.description:
Cron expression on when to run the job to recreate the access tokens for the vpp notifications.

Default runs at 5:00 AM on day 1 and 15 of every month. Beware that Spring Cron expressions contain seconds, but not every implementation of cron does. 

 ┌───────────── second (0-59) │ ┌───────────── minute (0 - 59) │ │ ┌───────────── hour (0 - 23) │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the month (1 - 31) │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── month (1 - 12) (or JAN-DEC) │ │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the week (0 - 7) │ │ │ │ │ │ (or MON-SUN -- 0 or 7 is Sunday) │ │ │ │ │ │ 0 0 5 1,15 * *

properties.name: relution.vpp.batch-retry-delay-multiplier-on-error
properties.description: The multiplier used to increase the batch retry delay for each retry attempt.

properties.name: relution.vpp.blocking-call-number-of-checks
properties.description:
The number of times to check if the blocking call completed.

When the call did not complete, wait for blockingCallWaitInterval.

properties.name: relution.vpp.blocking-call-thread-pool-size
properties.description: The number of threads to keep in the pool for blocking calls, even if they are idle.

properties.name: relution.vpp.blocking-call-wait-interval
properties.description:
The interval between checks to check if the blocking call finished.

totalTime = blockingCallWaitInterval * blockingCallNumberOfChecks

properties.name: relution.vpp.client-config.retry-count
properties.description:
Number of retries for ClientConfig calls, when apple replies with 5XX http code.

Less than 1 disables the feature.

properties.name: relution.vpp.client-config.retry-delay
properties.description: Delay before calling ClientConfig again after a retry.

properties.name: relution.vpp.connect-timeout
properties.description: The connection timeout.

properties.name: relution.vpp.core-thread-pool-size
properties.description: The number of threads to keep in the pool, even if they are idle.

properties.name: relution.vpp.initial-batch-retry-delay-on-error
properties.description: The initial amount of time to wait, before retrying a failed batch request, in milliseconds.

properties.name: relution.vpp.initial-sync-retry-delay-on-error
properties.description: The initial amount of time to wait, before retrying a failed batch request, in milliseconds.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-batch-error-count
properties.description: The maximum number of errors per batch request, after which sync is aborted.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-batch-retry-delay-on-error
properties.description: The maximum amount of time to wait, before retrying a failed batch request, in milliseconds.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-batch-retry-duration-on-error
