properties.name: relution.vpp.max-concurrent-batch-requests
properties.description: The maximum number of concurrent requests per VPP token.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-concurrent-requests
properties.description: The maximum number of concurrent requests.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-duplicate-pending-syncs
properties.description: The maximum number of duplicate pending sync requests per VPP token.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-in-memory-size
properties.description: The maximum number of bytes that can be buffered whenever the input stream needs to be aggregated. @see org.springframework.http.codec.ClientCodecConfigurer.ClientDefaultCodecs#maxInMemorySize(int)

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-sync-error-count
properties.description: The maximum number of total errors per VPP token, after which sync is aborted.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-sync-retry-count
properties.description: The maximum number of times to retry a failed sync.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-sync-retry-delay-on-error
properties.description: The maximum amount of time to wait, before retrying a failed batch request, in milliseconds.

properties.name: relution.vpp.max-sync-retry-duration-on-error
properties.description: The maximum amount of time for which to attempt to retry a batch request, in milliseconds.

properties.name: relution.vpp.notification-url
properties.description: Can be used to overwrite the default external URL for receiving notifications for VPP from Apple

properties.name: relution.vpp.read-timeout
properties.description: The read timeout.

properties.name: relution.vpp.remove-completed-tasks-older-than
properties.description:
The time after which completed tasks are removed from the server.

Any negative value disables this functionality.

properties.name: relution.vpp.requests.initial-retry-delay
properties.description:
The minimum amount of time to wait after a failed VPP request, before the request is scheduled for retry.

The actual request may be delayed further, if Apple's request quota would otherwise be exceeded.

properties.name: relution.vpp.requests.jitter-factor
properties.description: The jitter factor to apply to the retry delay, to avoid retry-storms.

properties.name: relution.vpp.requests.max-retry-attempts
properties.description: The maximum number of times a VPP request can be retried in case the initial request fails.

properties.name: relution.vpp.requests.max-retry-delay
properties.description:
The maximum amount of time to wait after a failed VPP request, before the request is scheduled for retry.

The actual request may be delayed further, if Apple's request quota would otherwise be exceeded.

properties.name: relution.vpp.requests.retry-delay-multiplier
properties.description: The multiplier used to increase the retry delay for each successive retry attempt.

properties.name: relution.vpp.resubscribe-for-failed-tokens-cron
properties.description:
Cron expression on when to resubscribe tokens with failed subscriptionState.

Default runs every day at 6:00 AM and 6:00 PM. Beware that Spring Cron expressions contain seconds, but not every implementation of cron does. 

 ┌───────────── second (0-59) │ ┌───────────── minute (0 - 59) │ │ ┌───────────── hour (0 - 23) │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the month (1 - 31) │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── month (1 - 12) (or JAN-DEC) │ │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the week (0 - 7) │ │ │ │ │ │ (or MON-SUN -- 0 or 7 is Sunday) │ │ │ │ │ │ 0 0 6,18 * * *

properties.name: relution.vpp.retry-delay
properties.description: The retry delay, in milliseconds.

properties.name: relution.vpp.service-config-refresh-interval
properties.description: Determines how often the {@link VppServiceConfigResponse VppServiceConfigResponse} is queried again from the server.

properties.name: relution.vpp.subscription-retries
properties.description: The number of retries, when subscribing to notifications failed.

properties.name: relution.vpp.subscription-retry-wait-interval
properties.description: The time to wait before retrying to subscribe.

properties.name: relution.vpp.sync-interval
properties.description: The automatic VPP synchronization interval.

properties.name: relution.vpp.sync-retry-delay-multiplier-on-error
properties.description: The multiplier used to increase the batch retry delay for each retry attempt.

properties.name: relution.wallpaper.image-magick.execution-timeout
properties.description: Timeout for executing the image magick command(s) using single threaded {@link ExecutorService}

properties.name: relution.wallpaper.image-magick.time-to-exit
properties.description: Maximum amount of time to wait before the process is terminated forcibly, in case it does not complete after being asked to shut down.

properties.name: relution.wallpaper.render-timeout
properties.description: Timeout for rendering an image using single threaded {@link ExecutorService}

properties.name: relution.webclient.repo-relution-io.close-timeout
properties.description: The maximum amount of time to wait for the incoming stream to close after write completes.

properties.name: relution.webclient.repo-relution-io.read-timeout
properties.description:
The maximum amount of time between incoming data packets, while reading.

If no further data is received for more than the specified timeout, the read operation is aborted.

properties.name: relution.webclient.repo-relution-io.write-timeout
properties.description:
The maximum amount of time between outgoing data packets, while writing.

If no further data can be written for more than the specified timeout, the write operation is aborted.

properties.name: relution.webclient.tcp.so-keep-alive
properties.description: When the keepalive option is set for a TCP socket and no data has been exchanged across the socket in either direction for 2 hours (NOTE: the actual value is implementation dependent), TCP automatically sends a keepalive probe to the peer. This probe is a TCP segment to which the peer must respond. One of three responses is expected: 1. The peer responds with the expected ACK. The application is not notified (since everything is OK). TCP will send another probe following another 2 hours of inactivity. 2. The peer responds with an RST, which tells the local TCP that the peer host has crashed and rebooted. The socket is closed. 3. There is no response from the peer. The socket is closed. The purpose of this option is to detect if the peer host crashes.

properties.name: relution.webclient.tcp.so-linger
properties.description: Specify a linger-on-close timeout. This option disables/enables immediate return from a close() of a TCP Socket. Enabling this option with a non-zero Integer timeout means that a close() will block pending the transmission and acknowledgement of all data written to the peer, at which point the socket is closed gracefully. Upon reaching the linger timeout, the socket is closed forcefully, with a TCP RST. Enabling the option with a timeout of zero does a forceful close immediately. If the specified timeout value exceeds 65,535 it will be reduced to 65,535.

properties.name: relution.webclient.tcp.tcp-keep-count
properties.description:
Keep-Alive retransmission maximum limit.

The value of this socket option is an {@code Integer} that is the maximum number of keep-alive probes to be sent. The socket option is specific to stream-oriented sockets using the TCP/IP protocol. The exact semantics of this socket option are system dependent.

When the {@link java.net.StandardSocketOptions#SO_KEEPALIVE SO_KEEPALIVE} option is enabled, TCP probes a connection that has been idle for some amount of time. If the remote system does not respond to a keep-alive probe, TCP retransmits the probe a certain number of times before a connection is considered to be broken. The default value for this keep-alive probe retransmit limit is system dependent, but is typically 8. The {@code TCP_KEEPCOUNT} option can be used to affect this value for a given socket.

properties.name: relution.webclient.tcp.tcp-keep-idle
properties.description:
Keep-Alive idle time.

The value of this socket option is an {@code Integer} that is the number of seconds of idle time before keep-alive initiates a probe. The socket option is specific to stream-oriented sockets using the TCP/IP protocol. The exact semantics of this socket option are system dependent.

When the {@link java.net.StandardSocketOptions#SO_KEEPALIVE SO_KEEPALIVE} option is enabled, TCP probes a connection that has been idle for some amount of time. The default value for this idle period is system dependent, but is typically 2 hours. The {@code TCP_KEEPIDLE} option can be used to affect this value for a given socket.

properties.name: relution.webclient.tcp.tcp-keep-interval
properties.description:
Keep-Alive retransmission interval time.

The value of this socket option is an {@code Integer} that is the number of seconds to wait before retransmitting a keep-alive probe. The socket option is specific to stream-oriented sockets using the TCP/IP protocol. The exact semantics of this socket option are system dependent.

When the {@link java.net.StandardSocketOptions#SO_KEEPALIVE SO_KEEPALIVE} option is enabled, TCP probes a connection that has been idle for some amount of time. If the remote system does not respond to a keep-alive probe, TCP retransmits the probe after some amount of time. The default value for this retransmission interval is system dependent, but is typically 75 seconds. The {@code TCP_KEEPINTERVAL} option can be used to affect this value for a given socket.

properties.name: relution.webclient.tcp.tcp-no-delay
properties.description: Disable Nagle's algorithm for this connection. Written data to the network is not buffered pending acknowledgement of previously written data.

properties.name: reset-password.reset-password-timeout
